tEn: Galactica: A Large Language Model for Science
tZh: Galactica：面向科学的大型语言模型
sEn: Galactica, a large language model for science, outperforms existing models on scientific tasks and sets new state-of-the-art on benchmarks like PubMedQA and MedMCQA.
sZh: Galactica 是一款面向科学的大型语言模型，在科学任务上超越现有模型，并在 PubMedQA 和 MedMCQA 等基准测试中创下新纪录。
absEn: Information overload is a major obstacle to scientific progress. The explosive growth in scientific literature and data has made it ever harder to discover useful insights in a large mass of information. Today scientific knowledge is accessed through search engines, but they are unable to organize scientific knowledge alone. In this paper we introduce Galactica: a large language model that can store, combine and reason about scientific knowledge. We train on a large scientific corpus of papers, reference material, knowledge bases and many other sources. We outperform existing models on a range of scientific tasks. On technical knowledge probes such as LaTeX equations, Galactica outperforms the latest GPT-3 by 68.2% versus 49.0%. Galactica also performs well on reasoning, outperforming Chinchilla on mathematical MMLU by 41.3% to 35.7%, and PaLM 540B on MATH with a score of 20.4% versus 8.8%. It also sets a new state-of-the-art on downstream tasks such as PubMedQA and MedMCQA dev of 77.6% and 52.9%. And despite not being trained on a general corpus, Galactica outperforms BLOOM and OPT-175B on BIG-bench. We believe these results demonstrate the potential for language models as a new interface for science. We open source the model for the benefit of the scientific community.
absZh: 信息过载是科学进步的主要障碍。科学文献和数据的爆炸式增长使得从大量信息中发现有用见解变得更加困难。如今，科学知识通过搜索引擎获取，但搜索引擎无法单独组织科学知识。在本文中，我们介绍了 Galactica：一种能够存储、组合和推理科学知识的大型语言模型。我们在一个大型科学语料库（包括论文、参考资料、知识库和许多其他来源）上进行训练。我们在一系列科学任务上超越了现有模型。在 LaTeX 方程等技术知识探针上，Galactica 以 68.2% 对 49.0% 的成绩超越了最新的 GPT-3。Galactica 在推理方面也表现出色，在数学 MMLU 上以 41.3% 对 35.7% 的成绩超越了 Chinchilla，在 MATH 上以 20.4% 对 8.8% 的成绩超越了 PaLM 540B。它还在下游任务（如 PubMedQA 和 MedMCQA dev）上分别以 77.6% 和 52.9% 的成绩创下了新的最先进水平。尽管没有在通用语料库上进行训练，Galactica 在 BIG-bench 上仍优于 BLOOM 和 OPT-175B。我们相信这些结果证明了语言模型作为科学新界面的潜力。我们开源该模型以造福科学界。

contrib.en: Introduces Galactica, a large language model trained on a curated scientific corpus of 106 billion tokens.
contrib.zh: 提出 Galactica，一个在 1060 亿 token 的精选科学语料上训练的大型语言模型。

contrib.en: Outperforms GPT-3 on LaTeX equation knowledge probes (68.2% vs 49.0%) and Chinchilla on mathematical MMLU (41.3% vs 35.7%).
contrib.zh: 在 LaTeX 方程知识探测上超越 GPT-3（68.2% 对 49.0%），在数学 MMLU 上超越 Chinchilla（41.3% 对 35.7%）。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art on PubMedQA (77.6%) and MedMCQA dev (52.9%) without fine-tuning.
contrib.zh: 在 PubMedQA（77.6%）和 MedMCQA dev（52.9%）上达到最先进水平，无需微调。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that repeated tokens improve both upstream and downstream performance on curated data.
contrib.zh: 证明在精选数据上重复 token 可同时提升上游和下游性能。

contrib.en: Introduces a working memory token (<work>) for step-by-step reasoning, outperforming larger models on MATH.
contrib.zh: 引入工作记忆 token（<work>）用于逐步推理，在 MATH 上超越更大模型。

contrib.en: Shows capability in multi-modal tasks including SMILES-to-IUPAC naming and protein function annotation.
contrib.zh: 展示多模态任务能力，包括 SMILES 到 IUPAC 命名和蛋白质功能注释。

limits.en: Corpus limited to open-access resources; closed-access knowledge could improve performance.
limits.zh: 语料限于开放获取资源；封闭知识可能提升性能。

limits.en: Self-imposed constraints on molecules (2 million) and proteins (0.5 million) limit modality performance.
limits.zh: 对分子（200 万）和蛋白质（50 万）的自设限制限制了模态性能。

limits.en: Effects of prompt pre-training vs core corpus not disentangled; general capabilities may rely on prompts.
limits.zh: 提示预训练与核心语料的效果未分离；通用能力可能依赖提示。

limits.en: Bias towards popular papers remains in citation prediction at 120B scale.
limits.zh: 在 120B 规模下，引文预测仍偏向热门论文。

limits.en: No notion of geometry (e.g., 3D coordinates) limits chemical and biological understanding.
limits.zh: 缺乏几何概念（如 3D 坐标）限制了化学和生物学理解。

items.en: Information overload is a major obstacle to scientific progress. The explosive growth in scientific literature and data has made it ever harder to discover useful insights in a large mass of information. Today scientific knowledge is accessed through search engines, but they are unable to organize scientific knowledge alone. In this paper we introduce Galactica: a large language model that can store, combine and reason about scientific knowledge. We train on a large scientific corpus of papers, reference material, knowledge bases and many other sources. We outperform existing models on a range of scientific tasks. On technical knowledge probes such as LaTeX equations, Galactica outperforms the latest GPT-3 by 68.2% versus 49.0%. Galactica also performs well on reasoning, outperforming Chinchilla on mathematical MMLU by 41.3% to 35.7%, and PaLM 540B on MATH with a score of 20.4% versus 8.8%. It also sets a new state-of-the-art on downstream tasks such as PubMedQA and MedMCQA dev of 77.6% and 52.9%. And despite not being trained on a general corpus, Galactica outperforms BLOOM and OPT-175B on BIG-bench. We believe these results demonstrate the potential for language models as a new interface for science. We open source the model for the benefit of the scientific community111galactica.org.
items.zh: 信息过载是科学进步的主要障碍。科学文献和数据的爆炸式增长使得在大量信息中发现有用见解变得更加困难。如今，科学知识通过搜索引擎获取，但它们无法单独组织科学知识。在本文中，我们介绍了Galactica：一种能够存储、组合和推理科学知识的大型语言模型。我们在一个包含论文、参考资料、知识库和许多其他来源的大型科学语料库上进行训练。在一系列科学任务上，我们优于现有模型。在诸如LaTeX方程等技术知识探测中，Galactica以68.2%对49.0%的成绩优于最新的GPT-3。Galactica在推理方面也表现出色，在数学MMLU上以41.3%对35.7%优于Chinchilla，在MATH上以20.4%对8.8%优于PaLM 540B。它还在下游任务如PubMedQA和MedMCQA开发集上分别以77.6%和52.9%的成绩创造了新的最先进水平。尽管没有在通用语料库上训练，Galactica在BIG-bench上优于BLOOM和OPT-175B。我们相信这些结果展示了语言模型作为科学新接口的潜力。我们开源该模型以造福科学界。

items.en: The original promise of computing was to solve information overload in science. In his 1945 essay "As We May Think", Vannevar Bush observed how "publication has been extended far beyond our present ability to make real use of the record" (Bush, 1945). He proposed computers as a solution to manage the growing mountain of information. Licklider expanded on this with the vision of a symbiotic relationship between humans and machines. Computers would take care of routine tasks such as storage and retrieval, "preparing the way for insights and decisions in scientific thinking" (Licklider, 1960).
items.zh: 计算的最初承诺是解决科学中的信息过载。在1945年的文章《诚如所思》中，Vannevar Bush观察到“出版已经远远超出了我们真正利用记录的能力”（Bush, 1945）。他提出计算机作为管理日益增长的信息山的解决方案。Licklider进一步扩展了这一愿景，提出了人与机器之间的共生关系。计算机将负责存储和检索等日常任务，“为科学思维中的洞察和决策铺平道路”（Licklider, 1960）。

items.en: Computing has indeed revolutionized how research is conducted, but information overload remains an overwhelming problem (Bornmann and Mutz, 2014). In May 2022, an average of 516 papers per day were submitted to arXiv (arXiv, 2022). Beyond papers, scientific data is also growing much more quickly than our ability to process it (Marx, 2013). As of August 2022, the NCBI GenBank contained 1.49×10121.49superscript10121.49\times 10^{12} nucleotide bases (GenBank, 2022). Given the volume of information, it is impossible for a single person to read all the papers in a given field; and it is likewise challenging to organize data on the underlying scientific phenomena.
items.zh: 计算确实彻底改变了研究的方式，但信息过载仍然是一个压倒性的问题（Bornmann and Mutz, 2014）。2022年5月，平均每天有516篇论文提交到arXiv（arXiv, 2022）。除了论文，科学数据的增长也远远快于我们处理它的能力（Marx, 2013）。截至2022年8月，NCBI GenBank包含1.49×10^12个核苷酸碱基（GenBank, 2022）。鉴于信息量之大，一个人不可能阅读某个领域的所有论文；同样，组织潜在科学现象的数据也具有挑战性。

items.en: Search engines are the current interface for accessing scientific knowledge following the Licklider paradigm. But they do not organize knowledge directly, and instead point to secondary layers such as Wikipedia, UniProt and PubChem Compound which organize literature and data. These resources require costly human contributions, for example writing a review of literature, an encyclopedia article or annotating a protein. Given this bottleneck, researchers continue to feel overwhelmed even with powerful search tools to hand.
items.zh: 搜索引擎是当前按照Licklider范式访问科学知识的接口。但它们不直接组织知识，而是指向诸如Wikipedia、UniProt和PubChem Compound等组织文献和数据的二级层。这些资源需要昂贵的人力贡献，例如撰写文献综述、百科全书文章或注释蛋白质。鉴于这一瓶颈，即使拥有强大的搜索工具，研究人员仍然感到不堪重负。

items.en: In this paper, we argue for a better way through large language models. Unlike search engines, language models can potentially store, combine and reason about scientific knowledge. For example, a model trained on the literature could potentially find hidden connections between different research, find hidden gems, and bring these insights to the surface. It could synthesize knowledge by generating secondary content automatically: such as literature reviews, encyclopedia articles, lecture notes and more. And lastly, it could organize different modalities: linking papers with code, protein sequences with compounds, theories with LaTeX, and more. Our ultimate vision is a single neural network for powering scientific tasks. We believe this is will be the next interface for how humans access scientific knowledge, and we get started in this paper.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们主张通过大型语言模型实现更好的方式。与搜索引擎不同，语言模型有可能存储、组合和推理科学知识。例如，在文献上训练的模型可能发现不同研究之间的隐藏联系，找到隐藏的瑰宝，并将这些见解带到表面。它可以通过自动生成二级内容来综合知识：例如文献综述、百科全书文章、讲义等。最后，它可以组织不同的模态：将论文与代码、蛋白质序列与化合物、理论与LaTeX等联系起来。我们的最终愿景是一个单一的神经网络来驱动科学任务。我们相信这将是人类访问科学知识的下一个接口，我们在本文中开始了这一工作。

items.en: We introduce a new large language model called Galactica (GAL) for automatically organizing science. Galactica is trained on a large and curated corpus of humanity’s scientific knowledge. This includes over 48 million papers, textbooks and lecture notes, millions of compounds and proteins, scientific websites, encyclopedias and more. Unlike existing language models, which rely on an uncurated crawl-based paradigm, our corpus is high-quality and highly curated. We are able to train on it for multiple epochs without overfitting, where upstream and downstream performance improves with use of repeated tokens.
items.zh: 我们介绍了一种名为Galactica（GAL）的新型大型语言模型，用于自动组织科学。Galactica在人类科学知识的大型精选语料库上训练。这包括超过4800万篇论文、教科书和讲义、数百万种化合物和蛋白质、科学网站、百科全书等。与依赖未精选的基于爬取的范式的现有语言模型不同，我们的语料库是高质量且高度精选的。我们可以在多个周期上训练而不发生过拟合，其中上游和下游性能随着重复标记的使用而提高。

items.en: Dataset design is critical to our approach, which includes curating a high-quality dataset and engineering an interface to interact with the body of knowledge. All data is processed in a common markdown format to blend knowledge between sources. We also include task-specific datasets in pre-training to facilitate composition of this knowledge into new task contexts. For the interface, we use task-specific tokens to support different types of knowledge. We process citations with a special token, that allows a researcher to predict a citation given any input context. We wrap step-by-step reasoning in a special token, that mimicks an internal working memory. And lastly, we wrap modalities such as SMILES and protein sequences in special tokens, which allows a researcher to interface with them using natural language. With this interface and the body of scientific knowledge in the model, we achieve state-of-the-art results across many scientific tasks.
items.zh: 数据集设计对我们的方法至关重要，包括策划高质量数据集和设计与知识体交互的接口。所有数据都以通用markdown格式处理，以在来源之间融合知识。我们还在预训练中包含特定任务的数据集，以促进将这些知识组合到新的任务上下文中。对于接口，我们使用特定任务标记来支持不同类型的知识。我们使用特殊标记处理引用，允许研究人员在任何输入上下文中预测引用。我们将逐步推理包装在特殊标记中，模拟内部工作记忆。最后，我们将SMILES和蛋白质序列等模态包装在特殊标记中，允许研究人员使用自然语言与它们交互。通过这个接口和模型中的科学知识体，我们在许多科学任务上取得了最先进的结果。

items.en: On reasoning tasks, Galactica beats existing language models on benchmarks such as MMLU and MATH (Hendrycks et al., 2020, 2021). With our reasoning token approach, we outperform Chinchilla on mathematical MMLU with an average score of 41.3% versus 35.7% (Hoffmann et al., 2022). Our 120B model achieves a score of 20.4% versus PaLM 540B’s 8.8% on MATH (Chowdhery et al., 2022; Lewkowycz et al., 2022). The 30B model also beats PaLM 540B on this task with 18 times less parameters. We believe this adds another reasoning method to the deep learning toolkit, alongside the existing chain-of-thought approach that has been well explored recently (Wei et al., 2022; Suzgun et al., 2022).
items.zh: 在推理任务上，Galactica在MMLU和MATH等基准测试上优于现有语言模型（Hendrycks et al., 2020, 2021）。通过我们的推理标记方法，我们在数学MMLU上以平均41.3%对35.7%优于Chinchilla（Hoffmann et al., 2022）。我们的120B模型在MATH上以20.4%对8.8%优于PaLM 540B（Chowdhery et al., 2022; Lewkowycz et al., 2022）。30B模型在参数少18倍的情况下也在该任务上击败了PaLM 540B。我们相信这为深度学习工具包增加了一种推理方法，与最近广泛探索的现有思维链方法相辅相成（Wei et al., 2022; Suzgun et al., 2022）。

items.en: We also find Galactica performs strongly in knowledge-intensive scientific tasks. We conduct detailed knowledge probes of Galactica’s knowledge of equations, chemical reactions and other scientific knowledge. Galactica significantly exceeds the performance of general language models such as the latest GPT-3 in these tasks; on LaTeX equations, it achieves a score of 68.2% versus the latest GPT-3’s 49.0% (Brown et al., 2020). Galactica also performs well in downstream scientific tasks, and we set a new state-of-the-art on several downstream tasks such as PubMedQA (77.6%) and MedMCQA dev (52.9%) (Jin et al., 2019; Pal et al., 2022).
items.zh: 我们还发现Galactica在知识密集型科学任务上表现强劲。我们对Galactica的方程、化学反应和其他科学知识进行了详细的知识探测。Galactica在这些任务上显著超过了通用语言模型（如最新的GPT-3）的性能；在LaTeX方程上，它以68.2%对49.0%的成绩优于最新的GPT-3（Brown et al., 2020）。Galactica在下游科学任务上也表现良好，我们在几个下游任务上创造了新的最先进水平，例如PubMedQA（77.6%）和MedMCQA开发集（52.9%）（Jin et al., 2019; Pal et al., 2022）。

items.en: We also demonstrate new capabilities with Galactica’s interface. First, the capability of predicting citations improves smoothly with scale, and we also find the model becomes better at modelling the underlying distribution of citations: the empirical distribution function approaches the reference distribution with scale. Importantly, we find this approach outperforms tuned sparse and dense retrieval approaches for citation prediction. This, along other results, demonstrates the potential for language models to replace the Licklider paradigm, document storage and retrieval, with their context-associative power in weight memory.
items.zh: 我们还展示了Galactica接口的新能力。首先，预测引用的能力随着规模平滑提高，我们还发现模型在建模引用的潜在分布方面变得更好：经验分布函数随着规模接近参考分布。重要的是，我们发现这种方法在引用预测方面优于调优的稀疏和密集检索方法。这一结果以及其他结果证明了语言模型在权重记忆中的上下文关联能力取代Licklider范式（文档存储和检索）的潜力。

items.en: In addition, Galactica can perform multi-modal tasks involving SMILES chemical formulas and protein sequences. We formulate drug discovery tasks as text prompts and show performance scales in a weakly supervised setup. We also demonstrate Galactica learns tasks such as IUPAC name prediction in a self-supervised way, and does so by attending to interpretable properties such as functional groups. Lastly, Galactica can annotate protein sequences with natural language, including predicting functional keywords.
items.zh: 此外，Galactica可以执行涉及SMILES化学式和蛋白质序列的多模态任务。我们将药物发现任务表述为文本提示，并显示性能在弱监督设置下随规模扩展。我们还展示了Galactica以自监督方式学习诸如IUPAC名称预测等任务，并通过关注可解释的属性（如官能团）来实现。最后，Galactica可以用自然语言注释蛋白质序列，包括预测功能关键词。

items.en: Galactica was used to help write this paper, including recommending missing citations, topics to discuss in the introduction and related work, recommending further work, and helping write the abstract and conclusion.
items.zh: Galactica被用于帮助撰写本文，包括推荐缺失的引用、引言和相关工作中要讨论的主题、推荐进一步的工作，以及帮助撰写摘要和结论。

items.en: LLMs have achieved breakthrough performance on NLP tasks in recent years. Models are trained with self-supervision on large, general corpuses and they perform well on hundreds of tasks (Brown et al., 2020; Rae et al., 2021; Hoffmann et al., 2022; Black et al., 2022; Zhang et al., 2022; Chowdhery et al., 2022). This includes scientific knowledge tasks such as MMLU (Hendrycks et al., 2020). They have the capability to learn in-context through few-shot learning (Brown et al., 2020). The capability set increases with scale, and recent work has highlighted reasoning capabilities at larger scales with a suitable prompting strategy (Wei et al., 2022; Chowdhery et al., 2022; Kojima et al., 2022; Lewkowycz et al., 2022).
items.zh: 近年来，LLM在NLP任务上取得了突破性性能。模型通过自监督在大型通用语料库上训练，并在数百个任务上表现良好（Brown et al., 2020; Rae et al., 2021; Hoffmann et al., 2022; Black et al., 2022; Zhang et al., 2022; Chowdhery et al., 2022）。这包括MMLU等科学知识任务（Hendrycks et al., 2020）。它们具有通过少样本学习进行上下文学习的能力（Brown et al., 2020）。能力集随着规模增加，最近的工作强调了在更大规模下通过合适的提示策略的推理能力（Wei et al., 2022; Chowdhery et al., 2022; Kojima et al., 2022; Lewkowycz et al., 2022）。

items.en: One downside of self-supervision has been the move towards uncurated data. Models may mirror misinformation, stereotypes and bias in the corpus (Sheng et al., 2019; Kurita et al., 2019; Dev et al., 2019; Blodgett et al., 2020; Sheng et al., 2021). This is undesirable for scientific tasks which value truth. Uncurated data also means more tokens with limited transfer value for the target use-case; wasting compute budget. For example, the PaLM corpus is 50% social media conversations, which may have limited transfer towards scientific tasks (Chowdhery et al., 2022). The properties of scientific text also differ from general text - e.g. scientific terms and mathematics - meaning a general corpus and tokenizer may be inefficient. We explore whether a normative approach to dataset selection can work with the large model paradigm in this work.
items.zh: 自监督的一个缺点是转向未精选的数据。模型可能反映语料库中的错误信息、刻板印象和偏见（Sheng et al., 2019; Kurita et al., 2019; Dev et al., 2019; Blodgett et al., 2020; Sheng et al., 2021）。这对于重视真理的科学任务来说是不可取的。未精选的数据也意味着更多标记对目标用例的迁移价值有限；浪费算力预算。例如，PaLM语料库中50%是社交媒体对话，这可能对科学任务的迁移价值有限（Chowdhery et al., 2022）。科学文本的属性也与通用文本不同——例如科学术语和数学——意味着通用语料库和分词器可能效率低下。我们在本文中探讨了规范性数据集选择方法是否适用于大型模型范式。

items.en: Works such as SciBERT, BioLM and others have shown the benefit of a curated, scientific corpus (Beltagy et al., 2019; Lewis et al., 2020a; Gu et al., 2020; Lo et al., 2019b; Gu et al., 2020; Shin et al., 2020; Hong et al., 2022). The datasets and models were typically small in scale and scope, much less than corpora for general models222One of the larger corpora S2ORC has <20absent20<20bn tokens, whereas corpora for GPT-3 and PaLM have ≥300absent300\geq 300bn tokens. ScholarBERT has a very large corpus at ¿200bn tokens, but the model is small at 770M capacity.. Beyond scientific text, Transformers for protein sequences and SMILES have shown potential for learning natural representations (Rives et al., 2021; Honda et al., 2019; Irwin et al., 2021; Nijkamp et al., 2022; Lin et al., 2022b). However, sequences like SMILES have descriptive limitations for representing chemical structure. We explore in this work whether a large, multi-modal scientific corpus can aid representation learning, where sequences occur alongside footprints and text in a signal-dense context.
items.zh: SciBERT、BioLM等工作展示了精选科学语料库的好处（Beltagy et al., 2019; Lewis et al., 2020a; Gu et al., 2020; Lo et al., 2019b; Gu et al., 2020; Shin et al., 2020; Hong et al., 2022）。数据集和模型通常在规模和范围上较小，远小于通用模型的语料库。除了科学文本，用于蛋白质序列和SMILES的Transformer已显示出学习自然表示的潜力（Rives et al., 2021; Honda et al., 2019; Irwin et al., 2021; Nijkamp et al., 2022; Lin et al., 2022b）。然而，像SMILES这样的序列在表示化学结构方面存在描述性限制。我们在本文中探讨了大型多模态科学语料库是否有助于表示学习，其中序列与足迹和文本一起出现在信号密集的上下文中。

items.en: The idea of "scaling laws" was put forward by Kaplan et al. (2020), who demonstrated evidence that loss scales as a power-law with model size, dataset size, and the amount of training compute. The focus was on upstream perplexity, and work by Tay et al. (2022a) showed that this does not always correlate with downstream performance. Hoffmann et al. (2022) presented new analysis taking into account the optimal amount of data, and suggested that existing language models were undertrained: "Chinchilla scaling laws". This work did not take into the account of fresh versus repeated tokens. In this work, we show that we can improve upstream and downstream performance by training on repeated tokens.
items.zh: “缩放定律”的概念由Kaplan等人（2020）提出，他们证明了损失随模型大小、数据集大小和训练算力量呈幂律缩放。重点在于上游困惑度，而Tay等人（2022a）的工作表明这并不总是与下游性能相关。Hoffmann等人（2022）提出了考虑最优数据量的新分析，并表明现有语言模型训练不足：“Chinchilla缩放定律”。这项工作没有考虑新鲜标记与重复标记。在本文中，我们表明可以通过在重复标记上训练来提高上游和下游性能。

items.en: Storing information in weights is more unreliable in the sense models may blend information together, hallucination, but it is more "pliable" in the sense it can associate information through the representation space, association. Despite hallucination risks, there is evidence large language models can act as implicit knowledge bases with sufficient capacity (Petroni et al., 2019). They perform well on knowledge-intensive tasks such as general knowledge (TriviaQA) and specialist knowledge (MMLU) without an external retrieval mechanism (Brown et al., 2020; Hendrycks et al., 2020).
items.zh: 将信息存储在权重中在某种意义上更不可靠，因为模型可能混合信息、产生幻觉，但在某种意义上更“可塑”，因为它可以通过表示空间关联信息。尽管存在幻觉风险，但有证据表明大型语言模型在足够容量下可以作为隐式知识库（Petroni et al., 2019）。它们在知识密集型任务（如通用知识TriviaQA和专业知识MMLU）上表现良好，无需外部检索机制（Brown et al., 2020; Hendrycks et al., 2020）。

items.en: The question of how to update network knowledge remains an active research question (Scialom et al., 2022; Mitchell et al., 2022). Likewise, the question of how to improve the reliability of generation is an active question (Gao et al., 2022). Despite these limitations, today’s large models will become cheaper with experience (Hirschmann, 1964), and so a growing proportion of scientific knowledge will enter weight memory as training and re-training costs fall. In this work we perform probes to investigate Galactica’s depth of knowledge, and show that the ability to absorb scientific knowledge improves smoothly with scale.
items.zh: 如何更新网络知识仍然是一个活跃的研究问题（Scialom et al., 2022; Mitchell et al., 2022）。同样，如何提高生成的可靠性也是一个活跃的问题（Gao et al., 2022）。尽管存在这些限制，今天的大型模型将随着经验而变得更便宜（Hirschmann, 1964），因此随着训练和重新训练成本的下降，越来越多的科学知识将进入权重记忆。在本文中，我们进行探测以研究Galactica的知识深度，并表明吸收科学知识的能力随着规模平滑提高。

items.en: Retrieval-augmented models aim to alleviate the shortcomings of weight memory. Examples of such models include RAG, RETRO and Atlas (Lewis et al., 2020b; Borgeaud et al., 2021; Izacard et al., 2022). These models have the advantage of requiring less capacity but the disadvantage of needing supporting retrieval infrastructure. Since knowledge is often fine-grained, e.g. the sequence of a particular protein, or the characteristics of a particular exoplanet, retrieval will likely be needed in future even for larger models. In this work we focus on how far we can go with model weights alone, but we note the strong case for using retrieval augmentation for future research on this topic.
items.zh: 检索增强模型旨在减轻权重记忆的缺点。此类模型的例子包括RAG、RETRO和Atlas（Lewis et al., 2020b; Borgeaud et al., 2021; Izacard et al., 2022）。这些模型具有需要更少容量的优点，但具有需要支持检索基础设施的缺点。由于知识通常是细粒度的，例如特定蛋白质的序列或特定系外行星的特征，即使对于更大的模型，未来也可能需要检索。在本文中，我们专注于仅使用模型权重能走多远，但我们注意到在未来的研究中强烈支持使用检索增强。

items.en: “Nature is written in that great book which ever is before our eyes – I mean the universe – but we cannot understand it if we do not first learn the language and grasp the symbols in which it is written." Galileo Galilei, The Assayer
items.zh: “自然写在那本永远在我们眼前的大书中——我指的是宇宙——但如果我们不首先学习其语言并掌握其书写符号，我们就无法理解它。”伽利略·伽利莱，《试金者》

items.en: The idea that Nature can be understood in terms of an underlying language has a long history (Galilei, 1623; Wigner, 1959; Wheeler, 1990). In recent years, deep learning has been used to represent Nature, such as proteins and molecules (Jumper et al., 2021; Ross et al., 2021). Amino acids are an alphabet in which the language of protein structure is written, while atoms and bonds are the language of molecules. At a higher level, we organize knowledge through natural language, and many works have trained on scientific text (Beltagy et al., 2019; Lewis et al., 2020a; Gu et al., 2020; Lo et al., 2019b). With Galactica, we train a single neural network on a large scientific corpus to learn the different languages of science.
items.zh: 自然可以用底层语言来理解的想法由来已久（Galilei, 1623; Wigner, 1959; Wheeler, 1990）。近年来，深度学习已被用于表示自然，例如蛋白质和分子（Jumper et al., 2021; Ross et al., 2021）。氨基酸是书写蛋白质结构语言的字母表，而原子和键则是分子的语言。在更高层次上，我们通过自然语言组织知识，许多工作已在科学文本上进行了训练（Beltagy et al., 2019; Lewis et al., 2020a; Gu et al., 2020; Lo et al., 2019b）。通过 Galactica，我们在一个大型科学语料库上训练单个神经网络，以学习科学的不同语言。

items.en: Our corpus consists of 106106106 billion tokens from papers, reference material, encyclopedias and other scientific sources. We combine natural language sources, such as papers and textbooks, and natural sequences, such as protein sequences and chemical formulae. We process LaTeX where we can capture it, and also include academic code to capture computational science. We highlight the corpus details in Table 1 and 2. Full details, including dataset components and filtering logic, are contained in the Appendix.
items.zh: 我们的语料库包含来自论文、参考资料、百科全书和其他科学来源的 1061061060 亿个 token。我们结合了自然语言来源（如论文和教科书）和自然序列（如蛋白质序列和化学式）。我们处理 LaTeX（在可捕获的情况下），并包含学术代码以捕获计算科学。我们在表 1 和表 2 中突出了语料库的细节。完整细节（包括数据集组件和过滤逻辑）包含在附录中。

items.en: Notably the dataset is small and curated compared to other LLM corpuses, which are larger and uncurated. This is a key question of this work: can we make a working LLM based on a curated, normative paradigm? If true, we could make more purposefully-designed LLMs by having a clear understanding of what enters the corpus, similar to expert systems which had normative standards (Jackson, 1990).
items.zh: 值得注意的是，与其他更大且未经整理的 LLM 语料库相比，该数据集较小且经过整理。这是本工作的一个关键问题：我们能否基于一个经过整理、规范的范式构建一个可用的 LLM？如果可行，我们就能通过清晰理解语料库中的内容来设计更有目的性的 LLM，类似于具有规范标准的专家系统（Jackson, 1990）。

items.en: Tokenization is an important part of dataset design given the different modalities present. For example, protein sequences are written in terms of amino acid residues, where character-based tokenization is appropriate. To achieve the goal of specialized tokenization, we utilize specialized tokens for different modalities:
items.zh: 鉴于存在不同的模态，分词是数据集设计的重要部分。例如，蛋白质序列以氨基酸残基的形式书写，此时基于字符的分词是合适的。为了实现专门分词的目标，我们为不同模态使用专门的 token：

items.en: Citations: we wrap citations with special reference tokens [START_REF] and [END_REF].
items.zh: 引用：我们用特殊引用 token [START_REF] 和 [END_REF] 包裹引用。

items.en: Step-by-Step Reasoning: we wrap step-by-step reasoning with a working memory token <work>, mimicking an internal working memory context.
items.zh: 逐步推理：我们用工作记忆 token <work> 包裹逐步推理，模拟内部工作记忆上下文。

items.en: Mathematics: for mathematical content, with or without LaTeX, we split ASCII operations into individual characters. Parentheses are treated like digits. The rest of the operations allow for unsplit repetitions. Operation characters are !"#$%&’*+,-./:;<=>?\^_‘| and parentheses are ()[]{}.
items.zh: 数学：对于数学内容（无论是否使用 LaTeX），我们将 ASCII 操作符拆分为单个字符。括号被视为数字。其余操作符允许不拆分重复。操作符字符为 !"#$%&'*+,-./:;<=>?\^_`|，括号为 ()[]{}。

items.en: Numbers: we split digits into individual tokens. For example 737612.62 -> 7,3,7,6,1,2,.,6,2.

SMILES formula: we wrap sequences with [START_SMILES] and [END_SMILES] and apply character-based tokenization. Similarly we use [START_I_SMILES] and [END_I_SMILES] where isomeric SMILES is denoted. For example, C(C(=O)O)N →→\rightarrow C,(,C,(,=,O,),O,),N.
items.zh: SMILES 公式：我们用 [START_SMILES] 和 [END_SMILES] 包裹序列，并应用基于字符的分词。类似地，对于异构体 SMILES，我们使用 [START_I_SMILES] 和 [END_I_SMILES]。例如，C(C(=O)O)N → C,(,C,(,=,O,),O,),N。

items.en: Amino acid sequences: we wrap sequences with [START_AMINO] and [END_AMINO] and apply character-based tokenization, treating each amino acid character as a single token. For example, MIRLGAPQTL -> M,I,R,L,G,A,P,Q,T,L.
items.zh: 氨基酸序列：我们用 [START_AMINO] 和 [END_AMINO] 包裹序列，并应用基于字符的分词，将每个氨基酸字符视为单个 token。例如，MIRLGAPQTL -> M,I,R,L,G,A,P,Q,T,L。

items.en: DNA sequences: we also apply a character-based tokenization, treating each nucleotide base as a token, where the start tokens are [START_DNA] and [END_DNA]. For example, CGGTACCCTC -> C, G, G, T, A, C, C, C, T, C.
items.zh: DNA 序列：我们也应用基于字符的分词，将每个核苷酸碱基视为一个 token，起始 token 为 [START_DNA] 和 [END_DNA]。例如，CGGTACCCTC -> C, G, G, T, A, C, C, C, T, C。

items.en: We cover a few of the specialized token approaches below that do not have clear parallels in the literature, in particular the working memory and citation tokens.
items.zh: 下面我们介绍一些在文献中没有明确对应关系的专门 token 方法，特别是工作记忆和引用 token。

items.en: Transformer-based architectures lack an explicit working memory capability, which means a single-forward pass has limited efficacy. This is problematic for tasks that require multiple steps of computation. A current workaround is using a Transformer’s output context as an external working memory to read from and write to. This is seen in recent work on chain-of-thought prompting (Wei et al., 2022; Suzgun et al., 2022). In one sense this is intuitive, as humans also augment their limited working memory with scratchpads. In another sense, we would like models to refine their representations internally like humans; e.g. mental arithmetic.
items.zh: 基于 Transformer 的架构缺乏显式的工作记忆能力，这意味着单次前向传播的效果有限。这对于需要多步计算的任务来说是个问题。当前的解决方法是使用 Transformer 的输出上下文作为外部工作记忆进行读写。这在最近的思维链提示工作中有所体现（Wei et al., 2022; Suzgun et al., 2022）。从某种意义上说，这是直观的，因为人类也会用草稿纸来增强有限的工作记忆。另一方面，我们希望模型像人类一样在内部优化其表示，例如心算。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Given a task like "What is the average of 43, 29, 51, 13?" a human can use internal or external working memory. In practice, they will use both symbiotically; meaning that working out that is written down in text is usually "missing" some steps performed internally.
items.capZh: 图 2：给定一个任务，如“43、29、51、13 的平均值是多少？”，人类可以使用内部或外部工作记忆。在实践中，他们会共生地使用两者；这意味着用文本写出的计算通常“缺少”一些内部执行的步骤。

items.en: There are two limitations with chain-of-thought. First, it relies on prompt discovery to find a prompt that elicits robust step-by-step reasoning; i.e. minimizes mistakes from doing too much in a single forward pass. Not only does this require finding a robust prompt that works in all cases, but it also often relies on few-shot examples which take up context space. What is worse, much of the step-by-step reasoning on the internet misses intermediate steps that a human has performed using internal memory. Humans do not write down every step they perform because it would lead to long and tedious answers. They write down the principal steps of reasoning, and do lower-level steps via internal working memory. This means there is "missing data" in written text, i.e. between written steps there are internal memory steps that are not explicitly stated.
items.zh: 思维链有两个局限性。首先，它依赖于提示发现来找到能引发稳健逐步推理的提示，即最小化单次前向传播中因执行过多而导致的错误。这不仅需要找到一个在所有情况下都有效的稳健提示，而且通常依赖于占用上下文空间的少样本示例。更糟糕的是，互联网上的许多逐步推理缺少人类使用内部记忆执行的中间步骤。人类不会写下他们执行的每一步，因为那样会导致冗长乏味的答案。他们写下推理的主要步骤，并通过内部工作记忆执行较低级别的步骤。这意味着书面文本中存在“缺失数据”，即在书面步骤之间存在未明确说明的内部记忆步骤。

items.en: Secondly, chain-of-thought prompting uses the neural network to perform tasks that it is arguably not best suited to doing; for example, arithmetic. Prior work has shown that accuracy on tasks like multiplication is proportional to term frequency (Razeghi et al., 2022). Given that classical computers are specialized for tasks like arithmetic, one strategy is to offload these tasks from the neural network to external modules. For example, prior work has looked at the possibilities of external tool augmentation, such as calculators (Thoppilan et al., 2022). However, this requires a strategy to identify where the neural network should offload; and it may not be straightforward when combined with a discovered zero-shot prompt, especially where lower-level computation steps are not explicitly stated in writing.
items.zh: 其次，思维链提示使用神经网络执行它可能并不最适合的任务，例如算术。先前的工作表明，乘法等任务的准确率与术语频率成正比（Razeghi et al., 2022）。鉴于经典计算机专门用于算术等任务，一种策略是将这些任务从神经网络卸载到外部模块。例如，先前的工作已经研究了外部工具增强的可能性，如计算器（Thoppilan et al., 2022）。然而，这需要一种策略来识别神经网络应在何处卸载；并且当与发现的零样本提示结合时，可能并不直接，尤其是在较低级别的计算步骤未在书面中明确说明的情况下。

items.en: Our solution is a working memory token we call <work>. We construct a few prompt datasets, see Table 3, that wrap step-by-by-step reasoning within <work> </work>. Some of these datasets were generated programmatically (OneSmallStep), by creating a problem template and sampling the variables, others were sourced online (Workout, Khan Problems), and others used existing datasets and transformed them into a <work> based context (GSM8k train). Where a computation is performed that a human could not do internally, we offload by writing and executing a Python script. An example is shown in Figure 3. Importantly, we do not have to turn this on, and the model can also predict the output from running a program. For our experiments, we did not find the need to turn Python offloading on, and leave this aspect to future work.
items.zh: 我们的解决方案是一个称为 <work> 的工作记忆 token。我们构建了几个提示数据集（见表 3），将逐步推理包裹在 <work> </work> 中。其中一些数据集是通过编程生成的（OneSmallStep），通过创建问题模板并采样变量；其他数据集来自在线来源（Workout, Khan Problems）；还有一些使用现有数据集并将其转换为基于 <work> 的上下文（GSM8k train）。当执行人类无法内部完成的计算时，我们通过编写和执行 Python 脚本来卸载。图 3 显示了一个示例。重要的是，我们不必启用此功能，模型也可以从运行程序预测输出。在我们的实验中，我们没有发现需要启用 Python 卸载，并将这方面留给未来的工作。

items.en: Longer term, an architecture change may be needed to support adaptive computation, so machines can have internal working memory on the lines of work such as adaptive computation time and PonderNet (Graves, 2016; Banino et al., 2021). In this paper, we explore the <work> external working memory approach as a bridge to the next step. Notably our <work> prompt datasets are not very large or diverse, so there are likely large further gains to be made with this approach.
items.zh: 长期来看，可能需要架构更改以支持自适应计算，使机器能够拥有内部工作记忆，类似于自适应计算时间和 PonderNet 等工作（Graves, 2016; Banino et al., 2021）。在本文中，我们探索了 <work> 外部工作记忆方法作为迈向下一步的桥梁。值得注意的是，我们的 <work> 提示数据集并不很大或多样化，因此这种方法可能还有很大的进一步改进空间。

items.en: A distinctive properties of academic text is citations. In order to represent the implicit citation graph within the text, we process citations with global identifiers and special tokens [START_REF] and [END_REF] signifying when a citation is made. Figure 4 shows an example of citation processed text from a paper.
items.zh: 学术文本的一个独特属性是引用。为了表示文本中的隐式引用图，我们使用全局标识符和特殊 token [START_REF] 和 [END_REF] 处理引用，以指示何时进行引用。图 4 显示了一篇论文中经过引用处理的文本示例。

items.en: We considered two type of citation identifier: (a) paper titles and (b) alphanumeric IDs. Based on ablations, we found that title based identifiers have greater citation prediction accuracy than IDs. However, we also found that paper titles are more prone to hallucination error at lower scales given the text-based nature of the identifier. We consider title processing for this paper, but we note the trade-offs between both approaches. Experiments for these ablations are contained in the Appendix.
items.zh: 我们考虑了两种类型的引用标识符：(a) 论文标题和 (b) 字母数字 ID。基于消融实验，我们发现基于标题的标识符比 ID 具有更高的引用预测准确率。然而，我们也发现，由于标识符基于文本，论文标题在较小规模下更容易出现幻觉错误。本文我们考虑标题处理，但我们注意到两种方法之间的权衡。这些消融实验的细节包含在附录中。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Prompt Pre-training. Pre-training weighs all tokens equally as part of the self-supervised loss. This leads to a weak relative signal for tasks of interest, meaning model scale has to be large to work. Instruction tuning boosts performance post hoc, and can generalize to unseen tasks of interest, but it risks performance in tasks that are distant from instruction set tasks. Prompt pre-training has a weaker task of interest bias than instruction tuning but less risk of degrading overall task generality.
items.capZh: 图5：提示预训练。预训练在自监督损失中平等地加权所有词元，导致目标任务信号相对较弱，因此模型规模必须足够大才能有效。指令微调事后提升性能，并能泛化到未见过的目标任务，但可能损害与指令集任务差异较大的任务性能。提示预训练的目标任务偏差弱于指令微调，但降低整体任务通用性的风险较小。

items.en: We deviate from existing language model research in one important direction, which is our decision to include prompts in pre-training alongside the general corpora. This is motivated by a number of observations.
items.zh: 我们在一个重要方向上偏离了现有语言模型研究，即决定在预训练中同时包含提示和通用语料。这一决定基于若干观察。

items.en: First, existing work has shown the importance of training token count on performance. The Chinchilla paper derived scaling "laws" taking into account number of tokens, training a 70bn model for 1.4 trillion tokens (Hoffmann et al., 2022). They obtained state-of-the-art performance on MMLU, beating much larger models such as Gopher (Rae et al., 2021).
items.zh: 首先，现有工作已证明训练词元数量对性能的重要性。Chinchilla论文推导了考虑词元数量的缩放"定律"，训练了70B模型在1.4万亿词元上（Hoffmann等，2022），在MMLU上取得了最先进性能，超越了Gopher等更大模型（Rae等，2021）。

items.en: Separately, research such as FLAN and T0 showed prompt tuning can boost downstream performance (Wei et al., 2021; Sanh et al., 2021; Chung et al., 2022). Their strategy involved converting tasks to text prompts, using prompt diversity in how the tasks are posed, and then fine-tuning on these prompt datasets. For FLAN and T0, this approach boosts performance, beating larger models such as GPT-3 on many tasks.
items.zh: 另外，FLAN和T0等研究表明提示微调可以提升下游性能（Wei等，2021；Sanh等，2021；Chung等，2022）。他们的策略包括将任务转换为文本提示，使用提示多样性来呈现任务，然后在这些提示数据集上微调。对于FLAN和T0，这种方法提升了性能，在许多任务上超越了GPT-3等更大模型。

items.en: And additionally there is the UnifiedQA approach (Khashabi et al., 2020). In this approach, a T5 model is fine-tuned on question answering datasets, and is shown to boost performance on out-of-domain question answering datasets (Raffel et al., 2020). The model outperforms GPT-3 on MMLU, a model 16 times larger.
items.zh: 此外还有UnifiedQA方法（Khashabi等，2020）。该方法在问答数据集上微调T5模型，并显示能提升域外问答数据集的性能（Raffel等，2020）。该模型在MMLU上超越了比它大16倍的GPT-3。

items.en: The first stream of research above focuses on total training tokens as a way to boost performance; i.e. it is token agnostic. The second stream of research focuses on task-context tokens as a way to boost performance; i.e. it is token selective. Since fine-tuned smaller models beat larger few-shot models on tasks like MMLU, this suggests world knowledge may be present in smaller models, but task-context knowledge may be poor given the relative number of task-context tokens seen in the general corpus.
items.zh: 上述第一类研究关注总训练词元作为提升性能的方式，即词元无关。第二类研究关注任务上下文词元作为提升性能的方式，即词元选择。由于微调后的小模型在MMLU等任务上超越了更大的少样本模型，这表明世界知识可能存在于小模型中，但任务上下文知识可能较差，因为通用语料中看到的任务上下文词元相对较少。

items.en: For this paper, we opt to augment pre-training data with more task prompts to boost performance at lower scales. This is advantageous if it obviates the need for more data scale, e.g. a >111 trillion corpus, or more model scale. The largest 120B model we train runs on a single NVIDIA A100 node. Additionally, given that fine-tuning requires expertise, making the model work out-the-box for popular tasks like question answering and summarization is more useful for users of the model. Lastly, by including prompts alongside general data, we maximize the generality of the model while boosting performance on some tasks of interest.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们选择用更多任务提示增强预训练数据，以在较小规模下提升性能。如果这能避免对更大数据规模（如超过111万亿词元的语料）或更大模型规模的需求，则是有利的。我们训练的最大120B模型在单个NVIDIA A100节点上运行。此外，鉴于微调需要专业知识，使模型开箱即用地适用于问答和摘要等流行任务对用户更有用。最后，通过在通用数据旁包含提示，我们最大化模型的通用性，同时提升某些目标任务的性能。

items.en: The closest analog to this approach for large language models is ExT5 (Aribandi et al., 2021). We take a similar approach by taking many machine learning training datasets, converting them to a text format, with prompt diversity, and then including them alongside general corpora in our pre-training set. A summary of prompt types is given in Table 4; the full details of datasets and prompts used are covered in the Appendix.
items.zh: 与这种方法最接近的大语言模型是ExT5（Aribandi等，2021）。我们采用类似方法，将许多机器学习训练数据集转换为文本格式，具有提示多样性，然后将其与通用语料一起包含在我们的预训练集中。提示类型总结见表4；数据集和提示的完整细节见附录。

items.en: Because of prompt inclusion, it is important to distinguish between in-domain performance, where the training dataset is included in pre-training, and out-of-domain performance, where the training dataset is not included in pre-training. We mark these results clearly in the Results section of this paper. Importantly, we do not advocate for prompt pre-training as an alternative to instruction tuning. In fact, instruction tuning on Galactica is likely useful follow-up work given its potential to boost performance on several tasks of interest.
items.zh: 由于包含提示，区分域内性能（训练数据集包含在预训练中）和域外性能（训练数据集不包含在预训练中）很重要。我们在本文的结果部分清晰标记了这些结果。重要的是，我们不主张将提示预训练作为指令微调的替代。事实上，在Galactica上进行指令微调可能是有用的后续工作，因为它有潜力提升若干目标任务的性能。

items.en: Galactica uses a Transformer architecture in a decoder-only setup (Vaswani et al., 2017), with the following modifications:
items.zh: Galactica采用仅解码器设置的Transformer架构（Vaswani等，2017），并进行以下修改：

items.en: GeLU Activation - we use GeLU activations for all model sizes (Hendrycks and Gimpel, 2016).
items.zh: GeLU激活——我们对所有模型规模使用GeLU激活（Hendrycks和Gimpel，2016）。

items.en: Context Window - we use a 2048 length context window for all model sizes.
items.zh: 上下文窗口——我们对所有模型规模使用2048长度的上下文窗口。

items.en: No Biases - following PaLM, we do not use biases in any of the dense kernels or layer norms (Chowdhery et al., 2022).
items.zh: 无偏置——遵循PaLM，我们在所有密集核或层归一化中不使用偏置（Chowdhery等，2022）。

items.en: Learned Positional Embeddings - we use learned positional embeddings for the model. We experimented with ALiBi at smaller scales but did not observe large gains, so we did not use it (Press et al., 2021).
items.zh: 学习位置嵌入——我们使用学习位置嵌入。我们在较小规模上实验了ALiBi，但未观察到显著收益，因此未使用（Press等，2021）。

items.en: Vocabulary - we construct a vocabulary of 50k tokens using BPE (Sennrich et al., 2015). The vocabulary was generated from a randomly selected 2% subset of the training data.
items.zh: 词汇表——我们使用BPE构建了50k词元的词汇表（Sennrich等，2015）。词汇表从随机选择的2%训练数据子集生成。

items.en: The different model sizes we trained, along with training hyperparameters are outlined in Table 5.
items.zh: 我们训练的不同模型规模及训练超参数见表5。

items.en: We train using AdamW with β1=0.9subscript𝛽10.9\beta_{1}=0.9, β2=0.95subscript𝛽20.95\beta_{2}=0.95 and weight decay of 0.10.10.1 (Loshchilov and Hutter, 2017). We clip the global norm of the gradient at 1.0, and we use linear decay for learning rate down to 10% of it value. We use dropout and attention dropout of p=0.1𝑝0.1p=0.1. We do not use embedding dropout. We found longer warmup was important for the largest model in the early stages of training to protect against the effects of bad initialization, which can have long-memory effects on the optimizer variance state and slow down learning. This may be specific to our model and training setup, and it is not clear whether this advice generalizes.
items.zh: 我们使用AdamW进行训练，β1=0.9，β2=0.95，权重衰减为0.1（Loshchilov和Hutter，2017）。我们将梯度的全局范数裁剪为1.0，并使用线性衰减将学习率降至其值的10%。我们使用dropout和注意力dropout，p=0.1。我们不使用嵌入dropout。我们发现，对于最大模型，在训练早期较长的预热对于防止不良初始化的影响很重要，不良初始化可能对优化器方差状态产生长记忆效应并减慢学习速度。这可能特定于我们的模型和训练设置，不清楚此建议是否具有普适性。

items.en: We use the metaseq library333https://github.com/facebookresearch/metaseq/ for training the models, built by the NextSys team at Meta AI.
items.zh: 我们使用metaseq库（https://github.com/facebookresearch/metaseq/）训练模型，该库由Meta AI的NextSys团队构建。

items.en: For training the largest 120B model, we use 128 NVIDIA A100 80GB nodes. For inference Galactica 120B requires a single A100 node. We choose the maximum model size to obey this constraint for downstream accessibility, and we will work to improve its accessibility for the research community in coming months.
items.zh: 对于训练最大的120B模型，我们使用128个NVIDIA A100 80GB节点。推理Galactica 120B需要单个A100节点。我们选择最大模型规模以满足此约束，以便下游可访问，并将在未来几个月努力提高其对研究社区的可访问性。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Repeated Tokens and Validation Loss. With four epochs of training, we continue to see validation loss fall for all model sizes. For the 120B model we see the first signs of overfitting at the beginning of the fifth epoch, and we early stop at this point.
items.capZh: 图6：重复词元与验证损失。经过四个周期的训练，所有模型规模的验证损失持续下降。对于120B模型，我们在第五个周期开始时首次看到过拟合迹象，并在此处提前停止。

items.en: We train the models for 450 billion tokens, or approximately 4.25 epochs. We find that performance continues to improve on validation set, in-domain and out-of-domain benchmarks with multiple repeats of the corpus.
items.zh: 我们对模型进行了4500亿个token的训练，约合4.25个epoch。我们发现，在验证集、领域内和领域外基准上，经过多次重复语料后，性能持续提升。

items.en: First, from Figure 6, validation loss continues to fall with four epochs of training. The largest 120B model only begins to overfit at the start of the fifth epoch. This is unexpected as existing research suggests repeated tokens can be harmful on performance (Hernandez et al., 2022). We also find the 30B and 120B exhibit a epoch-wise double descent effect of plateauing (or rising) validation loss followed by a decline. This effect becomes stronger with each epoch, and is most visible above with the 120B model towards end of training.
items.zh: 首先，从图6可以看出，经过四个epoch的训练，验证损失持续下降。最大的120B模型仅在第五个epoch开始时才开始过拟合。这出乎意料，因为现有研究表明重复token可能对性能有害（Hernandez等人，2022）。我们还发现30B和120B模型表现出逐epoch的双重下降效应，即验证损失先趋于平稳（或上升）后下降。这种效应随着每个epoch的进行而增强，在120B模型训练结束时最为明显。

items.en: To investigate further, we examine the per-source breakdown of validation loss to see if there is heterogeneity in loss behaviour. We plot example curves in Figure 23 overleaf for the 30B model. We see no signs of loss heterogeneity: loss falls for all sources. The 120B exhibits the same relative trend of declining validation loss for all sources until the beginning of fifth epoch, where all sources spike (see Appendix).
items.zh: 为了进一步研究，我们检查了验证损失的逐源分解，以观察损失行为是否存在异质性。我们在下页图23中绘制了30B模型的示例曲线。我们没有发现损失异质性的迹象：所有来源的损失均下降。120B模型在所有来源上表现出相同的相对趋势，即验证损失持续下降，直到第五个epoch开始时所有来源均出现尖峰（见附录）。

items.capEn: Figure 7: Validation Loss Per Source. Validation loss falls through training for all dataset categories. Results are shown for the 30B model above. The 120B exhibits the same relative trend of declining validation loss for all sources until the beginning of fifth epoch, where all sources spike (see Appendix).
items.capZh: 图7：逐源验证损失。所有数据集类别的验证损失在训练过程中均下降。上图展示了30B模型的结果。120B模型在所有来源上表现出相同的相对趋势，即验证损失持续下降，直到第五个epoch开始时所有来源均出现尖峰（见附录）。

items.en: The next question to answer is whether this trend extends to downstream performance and out-of-domain generalization. For this we use a 57 task subset of BIG-bench subset, a general corpus with principally non-scientific tasks and prompt types not included in pre-training (Srivastava et al., 2022). We plot results in Figure 8. We see no signs of overfitting suggesting that use of repeated tokens is improving downstream performance as well as upstream performance.
items.zh: 下一个要回答的问题是，这种趋势是否延伸到下游性能和领域外泛化。为此，我们使用了BIG-bench子集的57个任务，该子集是一个通用语料库，主要包含非科学任务和预训练中未包含的提示类型（Srivastava等人，2022）。我们在图8中绘制了结果。我们没有发现过拟合的迹象，这表明使用重复token不仅提升了上游性能，也提升了下游性能。

items.capEn: Figure 8: BIG-bench Performance During Training. The 57 task selection from BIG-bench contains principally non-scientific tasks. We use it as a proxy for out-of-domain performance. For the 120B model above, we see no signs of overfitting after four repeats of the corpus.
items.capZh: 图8：训练期间的BIG-bench性能。来自BIG-bench的57个任务选择主要包含非科学任务。我们将其用作领域外性能的代理。对于上述120B模型，在四次重复语料后，我们没有发现过拟合的迹象。

items.en: We suspect that two factors could be at play, a quality factor, the curated nature of the corpus enables more value per token to be extracted, or a modality factor, the nature of scientific data enables more value per token to be extracted. The missing step of causation is what leads specifically from either factor towards less overfitting, and we leave this question to further work. We note the implication that the "tokens→∞→tokens\text{tokens}\rightarrow\infty" focus of current LLM projects may be overemphasised versus the importance of filtering the corpus for quality.
items.zh: 我们推测可能有两个因素在起作用：一个是质量因素，即语料的精心策划使得每个token能提取更多价值；另一个是模态因素，即科学数据的性质使得每个token能提取更多价值。因果关系的缺失环节在于具体是哪个因素导致了更少的过拟合，我们将这个问题留给后续工作。我们注意到，当前LLM项目对“tokens→∞”的关注可能被过度强调，而过滤语料质量的重要性则被低估。

items.en: In the following sections, we turn to evaluating Galactica’s scientific capabilities. Specifically, we focus on the high-level design goals of building an LLM that can store, combine and reason about scientific knowledge - as these are needed for building a new interface for science.
items.zh: 在接下来的部分中，我们将评估Galactica的科学能力。具体来说，我们关注构建一个能够存储、组合和推理科学知识的LLM的高层设计目标——因为这些是构建科学新接口所必需的。

items.en: First, we examine how well Galactica absorbs scientific knowledge. We set up several knowledge probe benchmarks, building off the LAMA approach of Petroni et al. (2019). These were critical metrics during model development for identifying knowledge gaps within the corpus, and informing how to iterate the corpus. They also provide insight into the relative knowledge strengths of Galactica versus general language models, and we cover these results in this section before turning to the downstream tasks.
items.zh: 首先，我们考察Galactica吸收科学知识的能力。我们基于Petroni等人（2019）的LAMA方法，建立了几个知识探针基准。这些指标在模型开发过程中至关重要，用于识别语料中的知识空白，并指导如何迭代语料。它们还提供了Galactica与通用语言模型在相对知识优势方面的见解，我们在本节中介绍这些结果，然后再转向下游任务。

items.en: We construct a dataset of popular LaTeX equations from the fields of chemistry, physics, mathematics, statistics and economics. Memorisation of equations is useful to measure as it is necessary for many downstream tasks; for example, recalling an equation to use as part of an answer to a problem. Unless stated explicitly, Galactica results are reported as zero-shot. In total there are 434 equations we test for the knowledge probe.
items.zh: 我们构建了一个来自化学、物理、数学、统计学和经济学领域的流行LaTeX方程数据集。记忆方程对于衡量是很有用的，因为它是许多下游任务所必需的；例如，回忆一个方程以作为问题答案的一部分。除非另有说明，Galactica的结果为零样本报告。总共测试了434个方程用于知识探针。

items.en: We prompt with an equation name and generate LaTeX. An example is shown in Figure 9.
items.zh: 我们使用方程名称进行提示，并生成LaTeX。图9显示了一个示例。

items.en: We summarize the results in Table 6. Equation knowledge increases smoothly with scale. Galactica outperforms larger language models trained on general corpuses, indicating the value of a curated dataset.
items.zh: 我们在表6中总结了结果。方程知识随规模平滑增长。Galactica优于在通用语料上训练的更大语言模型，这表明了精心策划数据集的价值。

items.en: We also set up domain probes to track specialized knowledge for certain fields. We detail these below:
items.zh: 我们还设置了领域探针，以跟踪特定领域的专业知识。详情如下：

items.en: AminoProbe: a dataset of names, structures and properties of the 20 common amino acids.
items.zh: AminoProbe：一个包含20种常见氨基酸的名称、结构和性质的数据集。

items.en: BioLAMA: a dataset of biomedical factual knowledge triples.
items.zh: BioLAMA：一个生物医学事实知识三元组数据集。

items.en: Chemical Reactions: a dataset of chemical reactions.
items.zh: Chemical Reactions：一个化学反应数据集。

items.en: Galaxy Clusters: a dataset of galaxy clusters with their constellation classifications.
items.zh: Galaxy Clusters：一个包含星系团及其星座分类的数据集。

items.en: Mineral Groups: a dataset of minerals and their mineral group classifications.
items.zh: Mineral Groups：一个包含矿物及其矿物组分类的数据集。

items.en: In each case, we construct a prompt to test the knowledge. For example, for Chemical Reactions, we ask Galactica to predict the products of the reaction in the chemical equation LaTeX. We mask out products in the description so the model is inferring based on the reactants only. An example is shown in Figure 10.
items.zh: 在每种情况下，我们构建一个提示来测试知识。例如，对于化学反应，我们要求Galactica预测化学方程LaTeX中反应的产物。我们在描述中屏蔽了产物，以便模型仅基于反应物进行推断。图10显示了一个示例。

items.en: We report results for these knowledge probes in Table 7.
items.zh: 我们在表7中报告了这些知识探针的结果。

items.en: We also observe steady scaling behaviour in these knowledge probes, with the exception of BioLAMA which we suspect reflects zero-shot prompt difficulty for all LLMs. Notably fine-grained factual knowledge, such as "ConstellationOf(GalaxyCluster)" type-queries seems to scale smoothly with the size of the model.
items.zh: 我们在这些知识探测中也观察到稳定的缩放行为，但BioLAMA除外，我们怀疑这反映了所有LLM在零样本提示上的难度。值得注意的是，细粒度的事实知识，例如“ConstellationOf(GalaxyCluster)”类型的查询，似乎随模型规模平滑缩放。

items.en: We now turn to reasoning capabilities with the <work> token. We start by evaluating on the MMLU mathematics benchmarks, which we report in Table 8 (Hendrycks et al., 2020). Galactica performs strongly compared to larger base models, and use of the <work> token appears to boost performance over Chinchilla, even for the smaller 30B Galactica model.
items.zh: 现在我们转向使用<work>标记的推理能力。我们首先在MMLU数学基准上进行评估，结果报告在表8中（Hendrycks等人，2020）。Galactica相比更大的基础模型表现强劲，使用<work>标记似乎提升了相对于Chinchilla的性能，即使对于较小的30B Galactica模型也是如此。

items.en: We also evaluate on the MATH dataset to further probe the reasoning capabilities of Galactica (Hendrycks et al., 2021). We compare the <work> token prompt directly with the Minerva 5-shot chain-of-thought prompt mCoT for comparability. We report results in Table 9.
items.zh: 我们还在MATH数据集上评估，以进一步探测Galactica的推理能力（Hendrycks等人，2021）。我们直接将<work>标记提示与Minerva的5-shot思维链提示mCoT进行比较以确保可比性。结果报告在表9中。

items.en: We see that Galactica outperforms the base PaLM model by a significant margin, with both chain-of-thought and <work> prompts. Galactica 30B outperforms PaLM 540B on both prompts: an 18 times smaller model. This suggests Galactica may be a better base model for fine-tuning towards mathematical tasks.
items.zh: 我们看到Galactica显著优于基础PaLM模型，无论是使用思维链还是<work>提示。Galactica 30B在两种提示上都优于PaLM 540B：一个小18倍的模型。这表明Galactica可能是针对数学任务微调的更好基础模型。

items.en: We report Minerva results for completeness, which is a 540B PaLM fine-tuned towards LaTeX specifically. Minerva outperforms base Galactica, but the performance differences are non-uniform; which points towards different mathematical data biases. For a direct comparison to Minerva, the model is freely available for those who want to finetune Galactica towards LaTeX specifically as follow-up work.
items.zh: 我们报告Minerva的结果以保持完整性，Minerva是一个专门针对LaTeX微调的540B PaLM模型。Minerva优于基础Galactica，但性能差异不均匀；这指向了不同的数学数据偏差。为了与Minerva直接比较，该模型可免费获取，供那些希望将Galactica专门微调为LaTeX的人作为后续工作。

items.en: We now evaluate on downstream scientific tasks to see how well Galactica can compose its knowledge in different task contexts. We focus on knowledge-intensive scientific tasks and report full results in Table 10. For this we use the MMLU benchmark as well as some other popular scientific QA benchmarks. We include the MMLU results earlier without <work> to test for knowledge association specifically. Full MMLU results, including social sciences and other fields, are reported in the Appendix. We also perform data leakage analysis on these benchmarks for more confidence; results are in the Appendix.
items.zh: 我们现在在下游科学任务上评估，以了解Galactica在不同任务上下文中如何组合其知识。我们专注于知识密集型科学任务，并在表10中报告完整结果。为此，我们使用MMLU基准以及其他一些流行的科学问答基准。我们之前在不使用<work>的情况下包含了MMLU结果，以专门测试知识关联。完整的MMLU结果，包括社会科学和其他领域，在附录中报告。我们还对这些基准进行了数据泄漏分析以增加信心；结果在附录中。

items.en: From Table 10, Galactica can compose its knowledge into the question-answering task, and performance is strong; significantly outperforming the other open language models, and outperforming a larger model (Gopher 280B) in the majority of tasks. Performance against Chinchilla is more variable, and Chinchilla appears to be stronger in a subset of tasks: in particular, high-school subjects and less-mathematical, more memorization intensive tasks. In contrast, Galactica tends to perform better in mathematical and graduate-level tasks.
items.zh: 从表10可以看出，Galactica能够将其知识组合到问答任务中，性能强劲；显著优于其他开放语言模型，并在大多数任务中优于更大的模型（Gopher 280B）。与Chinchilla相比，性能变化更大，Chinchilla在部分任务中表现更强：特别是高中科目和较少数学、更多记忆密集型任务。相比之下，Galactica在数学和研究生级别任务中表现更好。

items.en: Our working hypothesis is that the Galactica corpus is biased towards graduate scientific knowledge, given it consists mostly of papers, which explains lagging performance in high-school subjects. While we do pick up some high-school level content through encyclopedias, textbooks and the filtered CommonCrawl, this amounts to a small quantity of tokens (a few billion). We leave the question of how to capture more of this base scientific knowledge in a curated way to future work.
items.zh: 我们的工作假设是，Galactica语料库偏向于研究生科学知识，因为它主要由论文组成，这解释了在高中科目上的滞后表现。虽然我们通过百科全书、教科书和过滤后的CommonCrawl获取了一些高中水平的内容，但这只占少量token（几十亿）。我们如何以策划的方式获取更多基础科学知识的问题留待未来工作。

items.en: On remaining tasks, we achieve state-of-the-art results over fine-tuned models at the time of writing. On PubMedQA, we achieve a score of 77.6% which outperforms the state-of-the-art of 72.2% (Yasunaga et al., 2022). On MedMCQA dev we achieve score of 52.9% versus the state-of-the-art of 41.0% (Gu et al., 2020). For BioASQ and MedQA-USMLE, performance is close to the state-of-the-art performance of fine-tuned models (94.8% and 44.6%) (Yasunaga et al., 2022).
items.zh: 在其余任务上，我们在撰写时达到了超越微调模型的最先进结果。在PubMedQA上，我们取得了77.6%的分数，优于最先进的72.2%（Yasunaga等人，2022）。在MedMCQA开发集上，我们取得了52.9%的分数，而最先进水平为41.0%（Gu等人，2020）。对于BioASQ和MedQA-USMLE，性能接近微调模型的最先进性能（94.8%和44.6%）（Yasunaga等人，2022）。

items.en: In this section we evaluate Galactica’s capability to predict citations given an input context, which is an important test of Galactica’s capability to organize the scientific literature. We find that both accuracy and the quality of distributional approximation improves with scale.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们评估Galactica在给定输入上下文的情况下预测引用的能力，这是对Galactica组织科学文献能力的重要测试。我们发现，准确性和分布近似的质量都随规模提升而提高。

items.en: We construct three datasets to evaluate the model’s capability to cite:
items.zh: 我们构建了三个数据集来评估模型的引用能力：

items.en: PWC Citations: a dataset with 644 pairs of machine learning concepts and papers that introduced them. Concepts consist of methods (e.g. ResNet) and datasets (e.g. ImageNet) from Papers with Code444https://paperswithcode.com.
items.zh: PWC Citations：一个包含644对机器学习概念及其引入论文的数据集。概念包括方法（例如ResNet）和数据集（例如ImageNet），来自Papers with Code。

items.en: Extended Citations: a dataset with 110 pairs of non-machine learning concepts and papers that introduced them. Examples of concepts include Kozac sequence and Breit-Wigner distribution.
items.zh: Extended Citations：一个包含110对非机器学习概念及其引入论文的数据集。概念示例包括Kozac序列和Breit-Wigner分布。

items.en: Contextual Citations: a dataset with 1,869 pairs of references and contexts from our arXiv validation set. The dataset is constructed by sampling 1,000 random references and collecting their contexts.
items.zh: Contextual Citations：一个包含1,869对参考文献及其上下文的数据集，来自我们的arXiv验证集。该数据集通过采样1,000个随机参考文献并收集其上下文构建。

items.en: For the PWC Citations and Extended Citations datasets, the citation prediction task is framed as a text generation task. The model is given a prompt like "In this paper we use ResNet method [START_REF]" in order to generate a prediction for the ResNet concept. For Contextual Citations, we prompt after the input context for the citation, where the context ends with [START_REF].
items.zh: 对于PWC Citations和Extended Citations数据集，引用预测任务被构建为文本生成任务。模型被给予类似“在本文中，我们使用ResNet方法[START_REF]”的提示，以生成对ResNet概念的预测。对于Contextual Citations，我们在输入上下文后提示引用，其中上下文以[START_REF]结尾。

items.en: We compare Galactica to sparse and dense retrieval-based approaches on this task.
items.zh: 我们将Galactica与基于稀疏和密集检索的方法在此任务上进行比较。

items.en: For the sparse baseline, we use ElasticSearch to create an index of all the references, including their titles, abstracts, and short snippets of text with the contexts they appear in. Then, given a text query, we retrieve the top references ordered by the sum of matching scores across all selected fields.
items.zh: 对于稀疏基线，我们使用ElasticSearch创建所有参考文献的索引，包括它们的标题、摘要以及它们出现的上下文的简短文本片段。然后，给定文本查询，我们检索按所有选定字段匹配分数之和排序的顶部参考文献。

items.en: For dense retriever baselines, we evaluate two different Contriever models (Izacard et al., 2021). The first is the pre-trained model released by Izacard et al. (2021). The second model we use is fine-tuned on a random subset of 10 million context/paper pairs from our corpus, trained to retrieve the right paper given a context before a citation. The setup for dense retrieval is: (1) each reference is encoded by the model using its title and abstract, (2) a text query is encoded by the same model, (3) the references that match the query re returned. Retrieval is performed using a FAISS index (Johnson et al., 2019).
items.zh: 对于密集检索基线，我们评估了两个不同的Contriever模型（Izacard等人，2021）。第一个是Izacard等人（2021）发布的预训练模型。第二个模型是在我们语料库中随机抽取的1000万个上下文/论文对上微调的，训练目标是给定引用前的上下文检索正确的论文。密集检索的设置是：（1）每个参考文献由模型使用其标题和摘要编码，（2）文本查询由同一模型编码，（3）返回与查询匹配的参考文献。检索使用FAISS索引进行（Johnson等人，2019）。

items.en: The performance on all evaluation sets increases smoothly with scale. At larger scales, Galactica outperforms the retrieval-based approaches as its context-associative power improves. This is an important result as current approaches for navigating the literature use these existing retrieval approaches. As the power of language models improves, we suspect they will become a valuable new tool for exploring the literature.
items.zh: 所有评估集上的性能随规模平滑提升。在更大规模下，Galactica优于基于检索的方法，因为其上下文关联能力提高。这是一个重要的结果，因为当前导航文献的方法使用这些现有的检索方法。随着语言模型能力的提升，我们怀疑它们将成为探索文献的有价值的新工具。

items.en: We now turn to look at how well Galactica can model the empirical citation distribution. For this analysis we use the Contextual Citations dataset, where prompts are extracted from a paper by taking the context before a citation as the prompt. An example prompt with a model prediction is shown overleaf in Figure 12.
items.zh: 现在我们来看Galactica对经验引用分布建模的效果。为此分析，我们使用上下文引用数据集，其中提示是从论文中提取的，具体做法是将引用前的上下文作为提示。一个带有模型预测的示例提示见下页图12。

items.en: We use the in-context citation data to analyse the distributional difference between predicted and ground truth paper counts. This allows us to assess the model bias towards predicting more popular papers. Specifically, for each context there is a ground truth and predicted reference. We count the number of times each reference appears in our corpus. We then compare the distribution of reference counts between the ground truth references and the predicted references using the Kolmogorov-Smirnov distance (Massey, 1951).
items.zh: 我们利用上下文引用数据来分析预测论文计数与真实论文计数之间的分布差异。这使我们能够评估模型偏向于预测更热门论文的程度。具体来说，对于每个上下文，都有一个真实引用和预测引用。我们统计每个引用在语料库中出现的次数。然后，我们使用Kolmogorov-Smirnov距离（Massey, 1951）比较真实引用与预测引用的引用计数分布。

items.en: The comparison between the citation count distributions for different model sizes can be seen in Figure 11. Figure 11(a) shows the decrease in the Kolmogorov-Smirnov distance between the distribution of ground truth paper citations and the distribution of predicted papers citations. Figure 11(b) shows how the distribution of paper counts for the predicted papers gets closer to the ground truth as the model size grows. At smaller scales the model is more prone to predicting more popular papers. As the model grows in size this bias towards predicting popular papers diminishes.
items.zh: 不同模型规模下的引用计数分布比较见图11。图11(a)显示了真实论文引用分布与预测论文引用分布之间的Kolmogorov-Smirnov距离随模型规模增大而减小。图11(b)显示，随着模型规模增大，预测论文的计数分布越来越接近真实分布。在较小规模下，模型更倾向于预测更热门的论文。随着模型规模增大，这种偏向热门论文的偏差逐渐减小。

items.en: We have studied Galactica’s scientific capabilities. It is perhaps not surprising that a specialist scientific model outperforms general models on scientific tasks, but what would be more surprising was if it outperformed general models on general NLP tasks. In this section, we show surprising evidence that it does just that.
items.zh: 我们研究了Galactica的科学能力。一个专门的科学模型在科学任务上优于通用模型或许并不令人惊讶，但更令人惊讶的是，如果它在通用NLP任务上也优于通用模型。在本节中，我们展示了令人惊讶的证据，表明它确实做到了这一点。

items.en: We evaluate on 57 BIG-bench tasks in Table 12 (Srivastava et al., 2022). The tasks are primarily non-scientific and test general language capability, for example anachronisms, figure of speech and metaphor boolean. We always evaluate with 5-shots, and we use the default prompt style from BIG-Bench. Importantly, we do not include this prompt style in pre-training; so the evaluation between Galactica and the other models is comparable 5-shot. Full details and results are in the Appendix. We summarize average scores in Table 12:
items.zh: 我们在表12中评估了57个BIG-bench任务（Srivastava et al., 2022）。这些任务主要是非科学性的，测试通用语言能力，例如时代错误、修辞手法和隐喻布尔值。我们始终使用5-shot评估，并采用BIG-Bench的默认提示风格。重要的是，我们在预训练中没有包含这种提示风格；因此Galactica与其他模型之间的评估在5-shot条件下是可比的。完整细节和结果见附录。我们在表12中总结了平均分数：

items.en: Both the 30B and 120B Galactica models outperform the larger OPT and BLOOM general models. This is a surprising result given we designed Galactica to trade-off generality for performance in scientific tasks.
items.zh: 30B和120B的Galactica模型都优于更大的OPT和BLOOM通用模型。考虑到我们设计Galactica是为了在科学任务中牺牲通用性以换取性能，这是一个令人惊讶的结果。

items.en: We suspect this result reflects the higher-quality of the Galactica corpus, stemming from the fact it is curated and also primarily academic text. Previous open LLM efforts likely overfocused on scale goals and underfocused on data filtering. Another implication is that the focus on tokens →∞→absent\rightarrow\infty from Chinchilla needs to be complemented with strong data quality procedures (Hoffmann et al., 2022). With this paper, we took an opposite approach by focusing on high-quality tokens and repeated epochs of training. However, the Chinchilla insight stands: and there is much more scientific text that we have not exploited in this work.
items.zh: 我们推测这一结果反映了Galactica语料库的高质量，这源于其经过筛选且主要来自学术文本。之前的开源LLM工作可能过度关注规模目标，而忽视了数据过滤。另一个启示是，Chinchilla中关注的token数量→∞需要与强大的数据质量流程相结合（Hoffmann et al., 2022）。在本文中，我们采取了相反的方法，专注于高质量token和重复的训练轮次。然而，Chinchilla的见解仍然成立：还有更多的科学文本我们尚未在本工作中利用。

items.en: We now turn to Galactica’s capability to interface with different scientific modalities. We start by looking at Galactica’s chemical capabilities. Chemical properties exhibit complex correlations which means the chemical space is very large. Better organization of chemical information through language models could aid chemical design and discovery. We explore how Galactica can provide a new interface for these tasks in this section.
items.zh: 现在我们转向Galactica与不同科学模态交互的能力。我们首先考察Galactica的化学能力。化学性质表现出复杂的相关性，这意味着化学空间非常大。通过语言模型更好地组织化学信息可以辅助化学设计和发现。在本节中，我们探讨Galactica如何为这些任务提供新的接口。

items.en: For this work, we only include a small subset of available compounds from PubChem Compound in pre-training. Specifically, we take a random subset (222 million) of total compounds (110110110 million). This is to ensure the model is not overly biased towards learning natural sequences over natural language. This is a constraint we can relax in future work, enabling for much larger corpus. Here we focus on the first step of investigating whether a single model can learn effectively in the multi-modal setting.
items.zh: 在本工作中，我们仅在预训练中包含了PubChem Compound中可用化合物的一小部分。具体来说，我们从总共1.1亿个化合物中随机选取了一个子集（2200万个）。这是为了确保模型不会过度偏向于学习自然序列而非自然语言。这是一个我们可以在未来工作中放松的约束，从而允许更大的语料库。这里我们专注于研究单个模型是否能在多模态设置中有效学习的第一步。

items.en: We find that a language model can learn chemical tasks such as IUPAC naming in a self-supervised way, and in addition, we can pose drug discovery tasks as natural language prompts and achieve reasonable results.
items.zh: 我们发现，语言模型可以以自监督方式学习化学任务，如IUPAC命名，此外，我们还可以将药物发现任务作为自然语言提示，并获得合理的结果。

items.en: SMILES is a line notation which represents chemical structure as a sequence of characters (Weininger, 1988). In the Galactica corpus, the SMILES formula occurs alongside information in the document, such as IUPAC names, molecular weight and XLogP. In the context of self-supervised learning, this means a language model is performing implicit multi-task learning: the model is predicting the next SMILES token, but can also use SMILES to predict other entities in the document.
items.zh: SMILES是一种行表示法，将化学结构表示为字符序列（Weininger, 1988）。在Galactica语料库中，SMILES公式与文档中的其他信息（如IUPAC名称、分子量和XLogP）一起出现。在自监督学习的背景下，这意味着语言模型在执行隐式的多任务学习：模型预测下一个SMILES token，但也可以利用SMILES来预测文档中的其他实体。

items.en: As an initial test, we set up a IUPAC Name Prediction task, where the task is to name a compound according to the IUPAC nomenclature given a SMILES formula input. The IUPAC nomenclature is a method of naming organic compounds that has a ruleset based on naming the longest chain of carbons connected by single bonds (Favre and Powerll, ). There is a large set of rules and the procedure is algorithmically complex, meaning it is hard to automate. As a result, it is missing from standard cheminformatics toolkits.
items.zh: 作为初步测试，我们设置了一个IUPAC名称预测任务，即给定SMILES公式输入，根据IUPAC命名法命名化合物。IUPAC命名法是一种命名有机化合物的方法，其规则基于命名由单键连接的最长碳链（Favre and Powerll, ）。规则集庞大且过程算法复杂，因此难以自动化。因此，标准化学信息学工具包中缺少这一功能。

items.en: Previous works such as STOUT and Struct2IUPAC have explored the possiblity of using RNNs and Transformers for this task (Rajan et al., 2021; Krasnov et al., 2021). We explore in this section whether Galactica can translate a SMILES specification to its IUPAC name in the self-supervised setting. We design a prompt based on the PubChem structure, with the SMILES as the only input, and the output to predict the IUPAC name.
items.zh: 之前的工作如STOUT和Struct2IUPAC已经探索了使用RNN和Transformer完成此任务的可能性（Rajan et al., 2021; Krasnov et al., 2021）。在本节中，我们探讨Galactica是否能在自监督设置中将SMILES规范翻译为其IUPAC名称。我们基于PubChem结构设计了一个提示，仅以SMILES作为输入，输出预测的IUPAC名称。

items.en: To evaluate, we use our compound validation set of 17,052 compounds, and prompt with the SMILES formula and predict the IUPAC name. To calculate accuracy, we use OPSIN to convert the generated IUPAC name to SMILES, canonicalize it and compare with the canonicalized SMILES target (Lowe et al., 2011).
items.zh: 为了评估，我们使用包含17,052个化合物的验证集，以SMILES公式作为提示，预测IUPAC名称。为了计算准确率，我们使用OPSIN将生成的IUPAC名称转换为SMILES，进行规范化，并与规范化的SMILES目标进行比较（Lowe et al., 2011）。

items.en: Accuracy increases smoothly with scale. Given we restricted the corpus to 2 million molecules, it is likely much better performance is achievable through training or fine-tuning on more molecules. The model is freely available for those who want to perform this follow-up work.
items.zh: 准确率随规模平滑增长。鉴于我们将语料库限制在200万个分子，很可能通过训练或微调更多分子可以获得更好的性能。该模型可免费提供给希望进行后续工作的人员。

items.en: The more immediate question is what is actually being learnt: is Galactica inferring names from the fundamental molecular structure? To answer this, we visualize the average atomic attention at each stage of a prediction in Figure 13 overleaf. Encouragingly, the results are interpretable in terms of the underlying chemistry, and Galactica attends to the correct group when predicting a name, e.g. for "amino" it attends primarily to the −\ce​N​H2\ce𝑁subscript𝐻2-\ce{NH_{2}} substituent.
items.zh: 更直接的问题是实际学到了什么：Galactica是否从基本分子结构中推断出名称？为了回答这个问题，我们在下页图13中可视化了预测每个阶段的平均原子注意力。令人鼓舞的是，结果在基础化学方面是可解释的，Galactica在预测名称时关注正确的基团，例如对于“amino”，它主要关注−NH₂取代基。

items.capEn: Figure 13: Attending to Functional Groups. Galactica uses its knowledge of chemistry to help with the IUPAC Naming task. At each stage of prediction, it attends to the part of the molecular graph associated with the group name, e.g. for "amino" it attends to the nitrogen atom; for thiazole, the sulphur atom.
items.capZh: 图13：关注官能团。Galactica利用其化学知识辅助IUPAC命名任务。在预测的每个阶段，它关注与基团名称相关的分子图部分，例如对于“amino”，它关注氮原子；对于“thiazole”，关注硫原子。

items.en: We now explore whether we can pose traditional drug discovery tasks in a natural language format, combining the different modalities involved. Humans organize knowledge via natural language, and so learning an interface between natural language and scientific modalities like SMILES could be a new tool for navigating the chemical space. We use MoleculeNet classification benchmarks to answer this question, which are summarized in Table 14 (Wu et al., 2017).
items.zh: 现在我们探讨是否可以将传统的药物发现任务以自然语言格式呈现，结合不同的模态。人类通过自然语言组织知识，因此学习自然语言与SMILES等科学模态之间的接口可能成为导航化学空间的新工具。我们使用MoleculeNet分类基准来回答这个问题，总结于表14（Wu et al., 2017）。

items.en: To evaluate, we include the training sets in pre-training by converting to a text format. We use prompt randomization (varying how the question is posed). For example, for BBBP the training prompt has forms like in Figure 14 below. These examples occur alongside the other corpuses in training, and each example is seen just over 444 times. This is not comparable to direct fine-tuning or supervision due to the presence of other data in pre-training, so it might be considered a form of weak supervision instead.
items.zh: 为了评估，我们将训练集转换为文本格式并包含在预训练中。我们使用提示随机化（改变问题的提出方式）。例如，对于BBBP，训练提示的形式如图14所示。这些示例在训练中与其他语料库一起出现，每个示例大约出现4次以上。由于预训练中存在其他数据，这无法与直接微调或监督相比，因此可能被视为一种弱监督形式。

items.en: For some MoleculeNet datasets, other modalities are implicitly present. For example, in the Tox21 dataset, bioassays concern particular receptors such as the androgen receptor (AR). As an experiment, we decided to frame the task in a text format with the protein sequence and the SMILES as part of the prompt. We show an example for Tox21 in Figure 15.
items.zh: 对于某些 MoleculeNet 数据集，其他模态隐式存在。例如，在 Tox21 数据集中，生物测定涉及特定受体，如雄激素受体（AR）。作为实验，我们决定将任务以文本格式呈现，将蛋白质序列和 SMILES 作为提示的一部分。我们在图 15 中展示了 Tox21 的一个示例。

items.en: We make sure to Kekulize the SMILES to be consistent with PubChem representations. For evaluation, we use the recommended splits from the DeepChem library (Ramsundar et al., 2019).
items.zh: 我们确保将 SMILES 凯库勒化，以与 PubChem 表示一致。对于评估，我们使用 DeepChem 库（Ramsundar 等人，2019）推荐的划分。

items.en: We present results in Table 15. Performance scales with model size. The scaling is slower than tasks like QA, and the base model lags a specialist model with explicit 3D information and 10 times more molecules (Zhou et al., 2022). We suspect the weak supervision setup is harder for this task, and fine-tuning and/or more molecule data is required to get sufficient task signal. The model is available for work on this.
items.zh: 我们在表 15 中展示了结果。性能随模型规模扩展。扩展速度比 QA 等任务慢，基础模型落后于具有显式 3D 信息和 10 倍以上分子的专家模型（Zhou 等人，2022）。我们怀疑弱监督设置对此任务更难，需要微调和/或更多分子数据才能获得足够的任务信号。该模型可用于此项工作。

items.en: For our purposes, the implication for future work is that we can learn drug discovery tasks via natural language prompts. If we can learn these relationships automatically in a signal-dense document context (e.g. online chemical databases), this might reduce the reliance on supervised datasets to perform these tasks.
items.zh: 就我们的目的而言，对未来工作的启示是，我们可以通过自然语言提示学习药物发现任务。如果我们能在信号密集的文档上下文（例如在线化学数据库）中自动学习这些关系，可能会减少对这些任务监督数据集的依赖。

items.en: As a final check, we can average Galactica’s attention heads across layers, and visualize whereabouts the model looks in the SMILES sequence to make a prediction (atomic attention). We show an example in Figure 16 for some Tox21 predictions.
items.zh: 作为最终检查，我们可以平均 Galactica 各层的注意力头，并可视化模型在 SMILES 序列中查看的位置以进行预测（原子注意力）。我们在图 16 中展示了一些 Tox21 预测的示例。

items.capZh: (c) 糠酸莫米松 (441336) 对 NR-AR

items.en: In this section we examine Galactica’s capability to interface with biological modalities. Language models could potentially play a role in automatic organisation of this data, for example annotating newly sequenced proteins with functional information. We explore the potential of this interface in this section.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们考察 Galactica 与生物模态交互的能力。语言模型可能在自动组织这些数据中发挥作用，例如用功能信息注释新测序的蛋白质。我们在本节中探索这种接口的潜力。

items.en: For protein sequences from UniProt, we include a small subset of available sequences in pre-training. Specifically, we take reviewed Swiss-Prot proteins; a high-quality subset (0.50.50.5 million) of total (227227227 million). This is to ensure the model is not overly biased towards learning natural sequences over natural language. As with molecule data, this is a constraint we can relax in future work, enabling for much larger corpus. Here we focus on the first step of investigating whether a single model can learn effectively in the multi-modal setting.
items.zh: 对于来自 UniProt 的蛋白质序列，我们在预训练中包含了可用序列的一小部分。具体来说，我们选取了经过审查的 Swiss-Prot 蛋白质；这是总序列（2.27 亿）中的一个高质量子集（50 万）。这是为了确保模型不会过度偏向于学习自然序列而非自然语言。与分子数据一样，这是一个我们可以在未来工作中放宽的约束，从而能够使用更大的语料库。这里我们专注于研究单个模型是否能在多模态设置中有效学习的第一步。

items.en: We find that a language model can learn an implicit measure of sequence similarity that it can use for tasks such as functional annotation and descriptions.
items.zh: 我们发现语言模型可以学习序列相似性的隐式度量，可用于功能注释和描述等任务。

items.capEn: Figure 17: Primary Structure Prediction. For three of the validation sets we observe smooth scaling, reflecting the potential for high sequence similarity with sequences in the training set; for example, orthologs in the case of the Paen validation set. The CASP set with sequence similarity constraints levels off, suggesting the gains from the 550k proteins in training quickly saturates for more out-of-domain sequences.
items.capZh: 图 17：一级结构预测。对于三个验证集，我们观察到平滑的扩展，反映了与训练集中序列高度相似的可能性；例如，Paen 验证集中的直系同源物。具有序列相似性约束的 CASP 集趋于平稳，表明来自训练中 55 万蛋白质的收益对于更多域外序列迅速饱和。

items.en: While Galactica does not explicitly model the 3D structure of a protein, the information needed for a specific conformation is contained in the linear amino acid sequence, which in turn determine function. As a first step, we test upstream performance through evaluating protein sequence perplexity. Constructing a good validation set is important and data leakage is a problem for works in this field. We construct four holdout sets to obtain more confidence about what is being learnt and what generalizes.
items.zh: 虽然 Galactica 没有显式建模蛋白质的 3D 结构，但特定构象所需的信息包含在线性氨基酸序列中，进而决定功能。作为第一步，我们通过评估蛋白质序列困惑度来测试上游性能。构建良好的验证集很重要，数据泄漏是该领域工作的问题。我们构建了四个保留集，以更确信所学内容和泛化情况。

items.en: First, we conduct BLAST on the sequences in the training set and remove all sequences with a sequence identity ≥50%absentpercent50\geq 50\% with 51 CASP14 target sequences. These are the same test sequences used in ESMFold (Lin et al., 2022b). In total we remove 167 sequences from the training set using this approach. We call this this holdout set CASPSimilarSeq. We call the 51 CASP14 target sequences CASPSeq.
items.zh: 首先，我们对训练集中的序列进行 BLAST，并移除所有与 51 个 CASP14 目标序列序列同一性 ≥50% 的序列。这些是与 ESMFold（Lin 等人，2022b）中相同的测试序列。我们总共使用这种方法从训练集中移除了 167 个序列。我们将此保留集称为 CASPSimilarSeq。我们将 51 个 CASP14 目标序列称为 CASPSeq。

items.en: Secondly, we conduct organism-level holdout, and remove all sequences from the Paenungulata clade of organisms, including elephants, elephant shrews, manatees and aadvarks. This allows us to test whether Galactica can annotate sequeces for organisms it has never seen before. In total we remove 109 sequences from the training set using this approach. We call this holdout set PaenSeq. Note that this does not enforce any sequence similarity constraints, and there may be very similar sequences in the training set.
items.zh: 其次，我们进行生物体级别的保留，并移除所有来自 Paenungulata 分支生物体的序列，包括大象、象鼩、海牛和土豚。这使我们能够测试 Galactica 是否能为从未见过的生物体注释序列。我们总共使用这种方法从训练集中移除了 109 个序列。我们将此保留集称为 PaenSeq。注意，这没有强制执行任何序列相似性约束，训练集中可能存在非常相似的序列。

items.en: Lastly, we conduct a randomized test split, consisting of 5456 sequences. There is no sequence identity constraint applied, so memorization may be more at play, but it still provides a signal about the breadth of sequence knowledge absorbed by the model. We call this holdout set UniProtSeq.
items.zh: 最后，我们进行随机测试划分，包含 5456 个序列。没有应用序列同一性约束，因此记忆可能更起作用，但它仍然提供了模型吸收的序列知识广度的信号。我们将此保留集称为 UniProtSeq。

items.en: We evaluate perplexity for all holdout sets in Table 16 and plot in Figure 17. For three of the validation sets we observe smooth scaling, reflecting the potential for high sequence similarity with sequences in the training set; for example, orthologs in the case of the Paen validation set. Interestingly, the CASP set with sequence similarity constraints levels off, suggesting the gains from the 550k proteins in training quickly saturates.
items.zh: 我们在表 16 中评估所有保留集的困惑度，并在图 17 中绘制。对于三个验证集，我们观察到平滑的扩展，反映了与训练集中序列高度相似的可能性；例如，Paen 验证集中的直系同源物。有趣的是，具有序列相似性约束的 CASP 集趋于平稳，表明来自训练中 55 万蛋白质的收益迅速饱和。

items.en: To investigate further, we example validation perplexity on the CASPSeq set during training of the 120B model, and we plot results in Figure 18 below.
items.zh: 为了进一步研究，我们在120B模型训练期间，在CASPSeq集上计算了验证困惑度，并将结果绘制在图18中。

items.capEn: Figure 18: CASPSeq Validation During Training. Overfitting occurs before the end of training, but the effect is not drastic, and repeating the protein sequences three times does not damage performance on this task. The final 120B model is the second-last point, reflecting the early stopping we applied (see earlier Sections)
items.capZh: 图18：训练期间的CASPSeq验证。过拟合发生在训练结束前，但影响并不显著，将蛋白质序列重复三次不会损害此任务的性能。最终的120B模型是倒数第二个点，反映了我们应用的早停（参见前文章节）。

items.en: We observe falling validation perplexity up until the start of the fourth epoch, at which point the model overfits for this particular dataset. This may suggest Galactica is getting worse at more "out-of-domain" proteins that differ significantly from the test set. For future work, less repetition is probably desirable; and more generally, increasing the diversity of proteins in the training dataset is likely to be beneficial.
items.zh: 我们观察到验证困惑度在第四轮开始前持续下降，此时模型在该特定数据集上出现过拟合。这可能表明Galactica在处理与测试集差异显著的“域外”蛋白质时表现变差。未来工作中，减少重复次数可能是可取的；更一般地，增加训练数据集中蛋白质的多样性可能是有益的。

items.en: We now look at specific translation capabilities from protein sequence toward natural language, which may be useful for tasks such as protein annotation. As a first test, we look at UniProt keywords that Galactica can infer from the sequence. An example of these is shown in Figure 20 overleaf.
