items.zh: 现在我们考察从蛋白质序列到自然语言的特定翻译能力，这可能对蛋白质注释等任务有用。作为初步测试，我们研究了Galactica能从序列中推断出的UniProt关键词。图20（见下页）展示了一个示例。

items.capEn: Figure 19: Protein Keyword Prediction. This test’s Galactica’s capability to predict protein keywords, e.g. "cytoplasm", from the sequence alone. For the Paen and General datasets, this capability improves smoothly with scale. It scales more slowly and begins to saturate for the CASPSimSeq set, reflecting the lower sequence similarity with sequences in the training set.
items.capZh: 图19：蛋白质关键词预测。本测试评估Galactica仅从序列预测蛋白质关键词（如“细胞质”）的能力。对于Paen和General数据集，该能力随规模平滑提升。对于CASPSimSeq集，提升速度较慢并开始饱和，反映了其与训练集中序列的相似性较低。

items.en: We report results in Table 17. F1subscript𝐹1F_{1} score increases across the holdout sets with scale, suggesting that Galactica can learn keywords by inferring from the sequence. However, we see saturation for the CASPSimSeq, suggesting this capability depends on how similar the sequences are to those in the training set. This is reflected in the example in Figure 20, where Galactica uses its knowledge of a similar proteins from different organisms, with a maximum sequence similarity of 91.8% in the training set, to help annotate.
items.zh: 我们在表17中报告了结果。F1分数随规模在所有保留集上增加，表明Galactica可以通过从序列推断来学习关键词。然而，我们在CASPSimSeq上观察到饱和，表明该能力取决于序列与训练集中序列的相似程度。这反映在图20的示例中，Galactica利用其对来自不同生物体的相似蛋白质（训练集中最大序列相似性为91.8%）的知识来帮助注释。

items.en: We attempted to visualize attention in the protein sequence, but we did not observe anything with biological intepretation (e.g. attention to domains). Our working hypothesis is that Galactica has learnt an implicit measure of sequence similarity that it uses to associate predicted keywords, but that this is not directly interpretable from where it attends to. This differs from our chemistry analysis where results were interpretable in terms of attention to the underlying atomic structure.
items.zh: 我们尝试可视化蛋白质序列中的注意力机制，但未观察到具有生物学解释性的现象（例如对结构域的注意力）。我们的工作假设是，Galactica学习了一种隐式的序列相似性度量，用于关联预测的关键词，但这无法直接从其注意力位置解释。这与我们的化学分析不同，后者在注意力机制对底层原子结构的关注方面是可解释的。

items.en: As the next test, we look at generating free-form descriptions of protein function from the sequence. We look at the UniProt function descriptions and compare to Galactica generated descriptions.
items.zh: 作为下一个测试，我们考察从序列生成蛋白质功能的自由形式描述。我们比较了UniProt功能描述与Galactica生成的描述。

items.en: We report results in Table 18. ROUGE-L score increases smoothly across all the holdout sets. We show an example overleaf in Figure 21 from PaenSeq. The protein is a Cytochrome b protein from a rock hyrax (Q7Y8J5). The closest sequence by similarity in the training set is a Cytochrome b protein from a pygmy hippopotamus (O03363) with 83% sequence similarity. In this case we get a perfect prediction from the description.
items.zh: 我们在表18中报告了结果。ROUGE-L分数在所有保留集上平滑提升。我们在下页图21中展示了来自PaenSeq的一个示例。该蛋白质是来自岩蹄兔的细胞色素b蛋白（Q7Y8J5）。训练集中相似性最高的序列是来自侏儒河马的细胞色素b蛋白（O03363），序列相似性为83%。在这种情况下，我们从描述中得到了完美的预测。

items.en: As with the keyword prediction task, Galactica appears to be learning based on matching sequences with similar ones it has seen in training, and using this to form a description. This suggests language models for protein sequences could serve as useful alternatives to existing search methods such as BLAST and MMseqs2 (Altschul et al., 1990; Steinegger and Söding, 2017).
items.zh: 与关键词预测任务类似，Galactica似乎基于将序列与训练中见过的相似序列进行匹配来学习，并利用这一点形成描述。这表明用于蛋白质序列的语言模型可以作为现有搜索方法（如BLAST和MMseqs2）的有用替代方案。

items.en: In this section we study the toxicity and bias of the Galactica model. We evaluate on benchmarks related to stereotypes, toxicity, and misinformation. We compare results to other language models. We find Galactica is significantly less biased and toxic than existing language models.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们研究Galactica模型的有毒性和偏见。我们评估了与刻板印象、有毒性和错误信息相关的基准。我们将结果与其他语言模型进行比较。我们发现Galactica的偏见和毒性显著低于现有语言模型。

items.en: For the following evaluations, we investigate Galactica’s ability to detect (and generate) harmful stereotypes and hate speech, using four widely used benchmarks.
items.zh: 在以下评估中，我们使用四个广泛使用的基准来研究Galactica检测（和生成）有害刻板印象和仇恨言论的能力。

items.en: CrowS-Pairs is a collection of 1,508 crowd-sourced pairs of sentences, one which is "more" stereotyping and one which is "less" stereotyping, and covers nine characteristics (Nangia et al., 2020). These characteristics are race, religion, socioeconomic status, age, disability, nationality, sexual orientation, physical appearance, and gender. A language model’s preference for stereotypical content is measured by computing the proportion of examples in which the "more" stereotypical sentence is preferred (as determined by log likelihood). Higher scores indicate a more harmfully biased model, whereas an ideal model with no bias would score 50%.
items.zh: CrowS-Pairs是一个包含1,508对众包句子的集合，其中一句“更”刻板，另一句“更不”刻板，涵盖九个特征（Nangia等人，2020）。这些特征包括种族、宗教、社会经济地位、年龄、残疾、国籍、性取向、外貌和性别。语言模型对刻板内容的偏好通过计算“更”刻板句子被偏好的比例（基于对数似然）来衡量。分数越高表示模型偏见越有害，而无偏见的理想模型得分为50%。

items.en: We report results for Galactica and other language models in Table 19. Galactica exhibits significantly lower stereotypical biases in most categories, with the exception of sexual orientation and age, when compared to the latest GPT-3 (text-davinci-002) and OPT 175B. Galactica attains a better overall score of 60.5% compared to the other models. Language models such as OPT use the Pushshift.io Reddit corpus as a primary data source, which likely leads the model to learn more discriminatory associations (Zhang et al., 2022). Galactica is trained on a scientific corpus where the incidence rate for stereotypes and discriminatory text is likely to be lower.
items.zh: 我们在表19中报告了Galactica和其他语言模型的结果。与最新的GPT-3（text-davinci-002）和OPT 175B相比，Galactica在大多数类别中表现出显著较低的刻板偏见，除了性取向和年龄。Galactica的整体得分为60.5%，优于其他模型。像OPT这样的语言模型使用Pushshift.io Reddit语料库作为主要数据源，这可能导致模型学习到更多歧视性关联。Galactica在科学语料库上训练，其中刻板印象和歧视性文本的发生率可能较低。

items.en: StereoSet aims to measure stereotypical biases across profession, religion, gender, and race (Nadeem et al., 2021). The benchmark contains two tasks: an intrasentence task and an intersentence task, with around 2,100 examples each in the development set.
items.zh: StereoSet旨在衡量职业、宗教、性别和种族方面的刻板偏见（Nadeem等人，2021）。该基准包含两个任务：句内任务和句间任务，每个任务在开发集中约有2,100个示例。

items.en: Intrasentence Task: the stereotype and associated context are in the same sentence.
items.zh: 句内任务：刻板印象和相关上下文在同一句子中。

items.en: Intersentence Task: the context and stereotype are in different (consecutive) sentences.
items.zh: 句间任务：上下文和刻板印象在不同（连续）句子中。

items.en: Alongside stereo- and anti-stereotypical variants of sentences, each example in StereoSet contains an unrelated sentence. This sentence is included for measuring a Language Modelling Score (LMS) and a Stereotype Score (SS). These two metrics are combined to form the Idealized Context Association Test score (ICAT), which is a balanced measure of bias detection and language modeling. An ideal, unbiased language model would score an LMS of 100, an SS of 50, and an ICAT of 100.
items.zh: 除了刻板和非刻板变体句子外，StereoSet中的每个示例还包含一个无关句子。该句子用于计算语言建模分数（LMS）和刻板分数（SS）。这两个指标结合形成理想化上下文关联测试分数（ICAT），这是偏见检测和语言建模的平衡度量。一个理想的无偏见语言模型应获得LMS为100，SS为50，ICAT为100。

items.en: We report results in Table 20. Galactica outperforms other models on all categories for the overall ICAT score.
items.zh: 我们在表20中报告了结果。Galactica在所有类别的整体ICAT分数上优于其他模型。

items.capEn: Figure 22: Toxicity rate on RealToxicityPrompts. Galactica exhibits much lower toxicity continuation rates, even as we increase the original prompt toxicity.
items.capZh: 图22：RealToxicityPrompts上的毒性率。即使增加原始提示的毒性，Galactica也表现出更低的毒性延续率。

items.en: To measure toxicity we use the RealToxicityPrompts (RTP) benchmark introduced in Gehman et al. (2020). We follow the same setup of Zhang et al. (2022) and sample 25 generations of 20 tokens using nucleus sampling (p=0.9) for each of 5000 randomly sampled prompts from RTP. We use the prompts to produce sequences (i.e, continuations) which are then scored by a toxicity classifier provided by Perspective API555https://github.com/conversationai/perspectiveapi.
items.zh: 为了衡量毒性，我们使用了 Gehman 等人 (2020) 提出的 RealToxicityPrompts (RTP) 基准。我们遵循 Zhang 等人 (2022) 的相同设置，对从 RTP 中随机抽取的 5000 个提示，使用核采样 (p=0.9) 生成 25 个长度为 20 个 token 的序列。我们使用这些提示生成序列（即续写），然后由 Perspective API 提供的毒性分类器进行评分。

items.en: TruthfulQA is a benchmark that measures answer truthfulness of language model generations (Lin et al., 2022a). It comprises 817 questions that span health, law, finance and other categories. We compare to other published language models. We report results in Table 21. Galactica exceeds the performance of other language models on this benchmark. However, absolute performance is still low. Given the curated nature of our corpus, this suggests that data alone does not cause language models to struggle at this task.
items.zh: TruthfulQA 是一个衡量语言模型生成答案真实性的基准 (Lin 等人, 2022a)。它包含 817 个问题，涵盖健康、法律、金融和其他类别。我们与其他已发表的语言模型进行比较。我们在表 21 中报告结果。Galactica 在该基准上的表现超过了其他语言模型。然而，绝对性能仍然较低。鉴于我们语料库的精心策划性质，这表明仅靠数据并不能导致语言模型在此任务上表现不佳。

items.en: We cover some of the limitations with work in this section.
items.zh: 我们在本节中讨论了本工作的一些局限性。

items.en: Our corpus has several limitations, both external and internally imposed. The main external constraint is our restriction to use open-access resources, and much of scientific knowledge like papers and textbooks are not open access. With access to these closed sources of knowledge, performance is likely to be considerably higher. We also use self-imposed constraints, like restricting the number of molecules and proteins for this work; without these constraints, we are likely to see considerable performance gains due to much larger corpuses for these modalities.
items.zh: 我们的语料库存在若干局限性，既有外部强加的，也有内部设定的。主要的外部约束是我们限制使用开放获取资源，而许多科学知识（如论文和教科书）并非开放获取。如果能够访问这些封闭的知识源，性能很可能会显著提高。我们还使用了自我施加的约束，例如限制本工作中分子和蛋白质的数量；如果没有这些约束，由于这些模态的语料库规模更大，我们很可能会看到显著的性能提升。

items.en: In several benchmarks, we show performance gains over existing language models, but we do not specifically disentangle the effects of the prompts we included in pre-training versus the core scientific corpus. In future work, we likely need to disentangle these effects in order to see whether general language capabilities are possible with a scientific corpus alone without prompt boosting.
items.zh: 在几个基准测试中，我们展示了相对于现有语言模型的性能提升，但我们没有具体区分预训练中包含的提示与核心科学语料库的影响。在未来的工作中，我们可能需要区分这些影响，以了解仅凭科学语料库（无需提示增强）是否可能实现通用语言能力。

items.en: While we demonstrate that the model approaches the true citation distribution with scale, some bias towards popular papers still remains with the 120B scale model, so the model likely requires augmentation before being used in a production environment.
items.zh: 虽然我们证明了模型随着规模扩大接近真实的引用分布，但 120B 规模的模型仍然对热门论文存在一定偏差，因此该模型在用于生产环境之前可能需要增强。

items.en: We opted for the former in this paper, but ideally we would need to explore what the latter could achieve, along the lines of the recent work of Chung et al. (2022). A limitation of this work is that we do not perform this direct comparison through ablations, making clear the trade-offs between approaches.
items.zh: 我们在本文中选择了前者，但理想情况下，我们需要探索后者可能实现的效果，类似于 Chung 等人 (2022) 的近期工作。本工作的一个局限性是我们没有通过消融实验进行直接比较，从而明确不同方法之间的权衡。

items.en: While Galactica absorbs broad societal knowledge through sources such as Wikipedia - e.g. 120B knows Kota Kinabalu is the capital of Malaysia’s Sabah state - we would not advise using it for tasks that require this type of knowledge as this is not the intended use-case.
items.zh: 虽然 Galactica 通过维基百科等来源吸收了广泛的社会知识——例如，120B 模型知道哥打京那巴鲁是马来西亚沙巴州的首府——但我们不建议将其用于需要此类知识的任务，因为这不是预期的用例。

items.en: While we have shown text-based Transformers are surprisingly powerful with text representations of scientific phenomena, we caution against the interpretation that text is all you need. For example, in chemistry, geometry is a fundamental language that determines meaning, yet Galactica has no notion of geometry; e.g. 3D co-ordinates of atoms.
items.zh: 虽然我们已经展示了基于文本的 Transformer 在科学现象的文字表示方面具有惊人的能力，但我们警告不要将“文本即一切”的解释。例如，在化学中，几何是决定含义的基本语言，但 Galactica 没有几何概念；例如，原子的 3D 坐标。

items.en: For development of the base model, we highlight several directions that may be worth pursuing.
items.zh: 对于基础模型的开发，我们强调几个可能值得探索的方向。

items.en: It is likely further gains can be obtained with mixture-of-denoising training as U-PaLM has recently shown (Tay et al., 2022b; Chung et al., 2022). We suspect this might be beneficial for the scientific modalities such as protein sequences, where the left-to-right LM objective is quite limiting.
items.zh: 通过混合去噪训练可能获得进一步的收益，正如 U-PaLM 最近所展示的那样 (Tay 等人, 2022b; Chung 等人, 2022)。我们怀疑这可能对科学模态（如蛋白质序列）有益，因为从左到右的语言模型目标在这些模态上相当受限。

items.en: We use a maximum context window length of 204820482048 tokens in this work. Extending this is likely to be beneficial for understanding in long-form scientific documents, such as textbooks and also documents with longer modality sequences (e.g. long protein sequences).
items.zh: 我们在本工作中使用最大上下文窗口长度为 2048 个 token。扩展这一长度可能有助于理解长篇科学文档，如教科书以及具有较长模态序列的文档（例如，长蛋白质序列）。

items.en: We cannot capture scientific knowledge adequately without capturing images. This is a natural follow-up project, although it likely requires some architectural modification to make it work well. Existing work such as Alayrac et al. (2022) has shown how to extend LLMs with this modality.
items.zh: 如果不包含图像，我们无法充分捕捉科学知识。这是一个自然的后续项目，尽管可能需要一些架构修改才能使其良好运行。现有工作如 Alayrac 等人 (2022) 已经展示了如何将这种模态扩展到 LLM。

items.en: We feel <work> could be a general-purpose reasoning token and we would like to invest more in this direction, including increasing prompt diversity and exploring performance on more benchmarks.
items.zh: 我们认为 <work> 可以成为一个通用推理 token，我们希望在这一方向上投入更多，包括增加提示多样性和探索更多基准上的性能。

items.en: Even as language models become more accurate with scale, we need assurances that their generations are correct and factual. Developing this layer is critical for production applications of language models in general beyond scientific applications.
items.zh: 即使语言模型随着规模扩大变得更加准确，我们也需要确保其生成的内容正确且符合事实。开发这一层对于语言模型在科学应用之外的通用生产应用至关重要。

items.en: Should we re-train from scratch to incorporate new scientific knowledge or train from older checkpoints? This is an open question, and further research is needed to find the best procedure for incorporating new knowledge into the model.
items.zh: 我们应该从头重新训练以融入新的科学知识，还是从旧的检查点继续训练？这是一个开放性问题，需要进一步研究以找到将新知识融入模型的最佳方法。

items.en: While we have shown how large language models can absorb large bodies of scientific knowledge, retrieval has a place for fine-grained types of knowledge, and we believe this is a strong direction to pursue to complement the flexible weight memory of the Transformer.
items.zh: 虽然我们已经展示了大型语言模型如何吸收大量科学知识，但检索在细粒度知识类型中仍有其用武之地，我们相信这是补充 Transformer 灵活权重记忆的一个有力方向。

items.en: For over half a century, the dominant way of accessing scientific knowledge has been through a store-and-retrieve paradigm. The limitation of this approach is the reasoning, combining and organization of information still relies on human effort. This has led to a significant knowledge throughput bottleneck. In this work we explored how language models might disrupt this paradigm and bring about a new interface for humanity to interface with knowledge.
items.zh: 半个多世纪以来，获取科学知识的主要方式一直是存储-检索范式。这种方法的局限性在于推理、组合和组织信息仍然依赖人类努力。这导致了显著的知识吞吐量瓶颈。在这项工作中，我们探索了语言模型如何颠覆这一范式，并为人类与知识的交互带来新的界面。

items.en: We showed that language models are surprisingly strong absorbers of technical knowledge, such as LaTeX equations and chemical reactions, and these capabilities tend to scale smoothly with model size. The context-associative power of language models likely confers significant advantages over search engines in the long-run. We demonstrated this for citation prediction, where a language model outperforms tuned sparse and dense retrieval pipelines for this task. Language models will likely provide a valuable new tool for exploring the literature and the body of scientific knowledge in coming years.
items.zh: 我们展示了语言模型在吸收技术知识（如 LaTeX 方程和化学反应）方面出奇地强大，并且这些能力往往随模型规模平滑扩展。语言模型的上下文联想能力可能长期内比搜索引擎具有显著优势。我们在引用预测任务中证明了这一点，语言模型在此任务上优于调优的稀疏和密集检索流程。语言模型很可能在未来几年为探索文献和科学知识体系提供有价值的新工具。

items.en: We also demonstrated that language models can compose a curated knowledge base to perform well in knowledge-intensive question answering tasks. This includes composing knowledge in a step-by-step reasoning manner. We showed that with a working memory token approach, we can achieve strong performance over existing methods on mathematical MMLU and MATH benchmarks. We suspect tasks like MATH are in principle solvable with language model approaches. The current bottleneck is the availability of high quality step-by-step datasets. However, language models will not perform these tasks like humans until they have an architectural change that supports adaptive computation.
items.zh: 我们还展示了语言模型可以组合精心策划的知识库，在知识密集型问答任务中表现良好。这包括以逐步推理的方式组合知识。我们展示了通过工作记忆 token 方法，在数学 MMLU 和 MATH 基准上取得了优于现有方法的性能。我们怀疑像 MATH 这样的任务原则上可以通过语言模型方法解决。当前的瓶颈是高质量逐步推理数据集的可用性。然而，语言模型在架构上支持自适应计算之前，不会像人类一样执行这些任务。

items.en: We also performed initial investigations on the potential of LLMs to act as a bridge between scientific modalities and natural language. We showed Galactica could learn tasks like IUPAC naming through self-supervision. We also showed that it is possible to formulate drug discovery tasks like MoleculeNet in a natural language prompt and achieve strong results without direct fine-tuning. Lastly, we showed the potential for tasks such as automatic protein annotation. In all, increasing the number (and size) of datasets that bridge between natural language and natural sequences is likely to boost performance further.
items.zh: 我们还初步研究了LLMs作为科学模态与自然语言之间桥梁的潜力。我们展示了Galactica可以通过自监督学习IUPAC命名等任务。我们还展示了可以在自然语言提示中制定如MoleculeNet这样的药物发现任务，并在无需直接微调的情况下取得强劲结果。最后，我们展示了自动蛋白质注释等任务的潜力。总之，增加连接自然语言与自然序列的数据集数量（和规模）可能会进一步提升性能。

items.en: Taken together, we feel there is a strong potential for language models to take on knowledge tasks that are currently human specialisms. We open source the models so others can build on our work, and we look forward to seeing how the open machine learning community will extend it.
items.zh: 综上所述，我们认为语言模型在承担目前人类专长的知识任务方面具有巨大潜力。我们开源了这些模型，以便其他人可以在我们的工作基础上继续发展，并期待看到开放机器学习社区如何扩展它。

items.en: Thanks to to Susan Zhang, Stephen Roller, Naman Goyal and others for their support in using metaseq. We build on the open LLM training foundation they made possible with the OPT project (Zhang et al., 2022).
items.zh: 感谢Susan Zhang、Stephen Roller、Naman Goyal等人在使用metaseq方面的支持。我们基于他们通过OPT项目（Zhang et al., 2022）实现的开放LLM训练基础进行构建。

items.zh: 感谢我们使用的库、数据集和其他工具的开源创建者。你们的努力加速了我们的工作；我们开源模型以加速你们的工作。
